layout.description: Three draggable, resizable panes with edge-snapping. Panes can be reordered in z-order by clicking. The canvas pane can be maximized to fill the viewport, causing toolbar and dock to float on top.

behavior.description: Begin dragging a pane by its title bar
Move pane to follow cursor. If any pane edge is within theme.sizes.snap_distance pixels of another pane's edge or the viewport boundary, snap to that edge and show a snap_preview colored guide line.
Drop the pane at its current position. Remove any snap preview guide lines.
Begin resizing a pane by its edge handle

Resize the pane by moving the dragged edge. Compute the delta from _resize_start to the current cursor position. For left/right edges, adjust the pane's left and/or width. For top/bottom edges, adjust top and/or height. Clamp to the pane's min_width/max_width and min_height constraints. Snap the edge to nearby pane edges within theme.sizes.snap_distance pixels.

children.description: Fixed-width (72px) vertical toolbar on the left side of the workspace. Contains a 2-column grid of 32x32px tool buttons followed by the fill/stroke color indicator widget. The pane can be dragged by its title bar, closed via the X button, and shown/hidden via the Window menu. Cannot be resized horizontally.
4-row, 2-column grid of 32x32px tool buttons. Single click selects the tool. Buttons with a small triangle indicator have alternate tools accessible via long-press (250ms hold).
Black arrow cursor for selecting, moving, and resizing objects. Click to select; Shift+click to add to selection; drag to move; drag handles to resize. No long-press alternates.

White arrow for selecting individual anchor points and path segments. Click an anchor to select it; drag to move it; drag control handles to adjust curves. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Partial Selection and Interior Selection tools. The button shows checked state when any tool in this slot is active.

items.label: Partial Selection
items.description: Select and move individual anchor points and path segments

items.label: Interior Selection
items.description: Select objects within groups without ungrouping

items.label: Magic Wand (Y)
items.description: Click an element to select every other element in the document that shares its fill color, stroke color, stroke weight, opacity, and blend mode within the Magic Wand Panel's tolerances. Shift+click adds to the selection; Alt+click subtracts.

children.description: Draw precise paths by clicking to place anchor points. Click-and-drag to create smooth bezier curves with control handles. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Pen, Add Anchor Point, Delete Anchor Point, and Anchor Point tools.

items.label: Pen
items.description: Draw paths by clicking to place anchor points

items.label: Add Anchor Point
items.description: Add anchor points to an existing path

items.label: Delete Anchor Point
items.description: Remove anchor points from a path

items.label: Anchor Point
items.description: Convert anchor points between smooth and corner

children.description: Freehand drawing tool that creates paths by tracking mouse movement. The path is smoothed after drawing. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Pencil, Path Eraser, and Smooth tools.

items.label: Pencil
items.description: Draw freehand paths

items.label: Path Eraser
items.description: Erase portions of paths

items.label: Smooth
items.description: Smooth rough path segments

children.description: Create and edit text objects. Click on the canvas to place a text insertion point; click-drag to create a text area box. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Type and Type on a Path tools.

items.label: Type
items.description: Create and edit text

items.label: Type on a Path
items.description: Place text along a path curve

children.description: Draw straight line segments. Click to place the start point, drag to the end point, and release. Hold Shift to constrain to 45-degree increments. No long-press alternates.

Draw rectangular shapes. Click-drag to define the bounding box. Hold Shift to constrain to a square; hold Alt/Option to draw from center. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Rectangle, Rounded Rectangle, Polygon, and Star tools.

items.label: Rectangle
items.description: Draw rectangles and squares

items.label: Rounded Rectangle
items.description: Draw rectangles with rounded corners

items.label: Ellipse
items.description: Draw ellipses and circles

items.label: Polygon
items.description: Draw regular polygons

items.label: Star
items.description: Draw star shapes

children.description: Freehand selection tool. Draw a closed lasso boundary around objects to select them. Any object whose bounds intersect the lasso path is included in the selection. No long-press alternates.

Pan the canvas by click-and-drag. Spacebar held while another tool is active temporarily switches to Hand. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Hand and Zoom tools (the navigation tool pair). Double-click the icon to invoke fit_active_artboard. The button shows checked state when either tool in this slot is active. See HAND_TOOL.md and ZOOM_TOOL.md.

items.label: Hand (H)
items.description: Pan the canvas by click-and-drag

items.label: Zoom (Z)
items.description: Zoom in by click; Alt-click to zoom out; drag for scrubby or marquee zoom; Ctrl+wheel for stepped zoom

children.description: Create, activate, move, resize, and duplicate artboards on the canvas. Drag from empty canvas to create a new artboard. Click an artboard to activate it (writes panel-selection per the panel rules). Drag an artboard interior to move it; drag a resize handle to resize it; Alt-drag to duplicate. Double- click an artboard to open the Artboard Options Dialogue. Double-click the icon to invoke fit_all_artboards. No long-press alternates. See ARTBOARD_TOOL.md.

Central drawing area with a multi-tab document bar at the top. Each open document gets a tab labeled with its filename (or "Untitled-N" for new documents); a * prefix indicates unsaved changes. Clicking a tab switches the visible canvas. The drawing surface supports zoom (Ctrl+=/-, scroll wheel) and pan (scroll bars, Space+drag). The pane is a flex pane that expands to fill space between the toolbar and dock. Double-clicking the title bar maximizes the canvas to fill the viewport; double-click again or click the maximize button to restore.

Horizontal row of tab buttons at the top of the canvas pane. Starts empty; tabs are added dynamically by new_document and open_file actions via create_child. Each tab shows the document name and a close (X) button. Clicking a tab sets active_tab to that tab's index; the active tab gets highlighted styling. The tab bar is hidden when no documents are open (tab_count == 0).

Container for document canvases, filling the space below the tab bar. Each open document gets a canvas element here via create_child (see actions.yaml::new_document, ::open_file). Only the canvas matching active_tab is visible; the rest are hidden. Starts empty — the brand logo below acts as the visual placeholder until the first document is opened. The visible canvas is the interactive drawing surface where tools operate; canvas_bootstrap.mjs binds a Model (with undo/redo) to each new canvas-stack as it appears in the DOM and the engine renders the matching document into its 5-layer SVG stack.

Brand logo shown in the top-right of the drawing surface when no documents are open. Spans 2 Bootstrap columns wide (16.67%) and is positioned in the top-right corner. Hidden once any document tab is open.

Right-side anchored dock pane containing a dock_view element. The dock_view renders panel groups with tab bars, collapse/expand chevrons, and hamburger menus automatically from its groups list. When collapsed, shows a narrow icon strip. Panel groups can be dragged out to create floating dock panes, or dragged between docks. The dock is resizable between 150px and 500px. Panel group state is saved with workspace layouts.

content.description: The primary anchored dock containing all panel groups. The dock_view element renders group headers (tab bars with panel tabs, collapse chevrons, hamburger menus), group bodies (active panel content from workspace/panels/*.yaml), group separators, and the collapsed icon strip. Panel tabs can be dragged to reorder within a group, move to another group's tab bar, or dropped on an empty area of a dock to create a new panel group at that position. Dragging a tab or group to empty space outside any dock creates a new floating dock pane. Groups can be dragged by their grip handle to reorder within the dock or detach as floating dock panes. Empty groups are removed automatically when their last panel is moved or closed. Empty floating docks are removed when their last group is emptied. Double-clicking a floating dock's title bar merges its groups back into this anchored dock.

items.label: Workspace
items.description: Lists saved workspace layouts with a checkmark on the active one. Includes Save As, Reset to Default, and Revert to Saved commands.

items.label: Appearance
items.description: Lists available UI appearances (color themes) with a checkmark on the active one. Predefined appearances are loaded from workspace/appearances/*.json. Users can save custom appearances via Save As.

active_document.description: Properties of the currently active document tab. When no tab is open (active_tab == -1), all properties resolve to their defaults.

is_modified.description: The active document has unsaved changes

has_filename.description: The active document has been saved to a file at least once

filename.description: The active document's filename (empty string if untitled)

any_modified.description: Any open document has unsaved changes

has_selection.description: One or more objects are selected

zoom_level.description: Current zoom factor (1.0 = 100%). Per-document; not serialized to disk. Clamped at the producer side to [preferences.viewport.min_zoom, preferences.viewport.max_zoom].

view_offset_x.description: Pan: screen-space pixel coordinate of the document's (0,0) point within the canvas widget. Positive moves the document origin rightward in the viewport. Per-document; not serialized.

view_offset_y.description: Pan: screen-space pixel coordinate of the document's (0,0) point within the canvas widget. Positive moves the document origin downward. Per-document; not serialized.

layers_panel_selection_count.description: Number of items selected in the layers panel

layers_panel_selection_is_container.description: True when the sole selected layer item is a container (group or layer)

layers_panel_selection_has_group.description: True when at least one selected layer item is a group

element_tree.description: Hierarchical tree of document elements for panel rendering (tree_view source)

element_selection.description: List of IDs of elements currently selected on the canvas

top_level_layers.description: Top-level Layer elements as flat dicts with keys {kind, name, common: {visibility, locked}, path}. Used by bulk-toggle actions (toggle_all_layers_*) to iterate.

top_level_layer_paths.description: Paths of the top-level Layer elements, one entry per Layer in document order. Used as the source for bulk foreach loops.

next_layer_name.description: Auto-generated layer name of the form "Layer N" where N is the smallest positive integer not already in use by another top-level layer.

new_layer_insert_index.description: Index at which a newly created layer should be inserted — one above the topmost panel-selected layer, or the current layer count when nothing is selected.

workspace.description: Properties of the workspace layout system, managed by the layout save/load engine.

has_saved_layout.description: A named layout is currently active (was loaded or saved)

active_layout_name.description: Name of the currently active layout (empty if none)

panel.description: Active panel state. Resolves to the state of whichever panel is currently focused or is the target of the effect being evaluated. Properties map to keys stored in the StateStore's panel-scoped buckets; each panel contributes its own subset.

layers_panel_selection.description: Paths of items currently selected in the layers panel tree. Driven by panel-local selection state; serialized as __path__ markers so they round-trip through the expression evaluator.

isolation_stack.description: Stack of paths to isolated containers in the layers panel, innermost level last. Pushed by enter_isolation_mode, popped by exit_isolation_mode.

recent_colors.description: Per-document list of recently-committed colors, newest first, capped at 10 entries.

event.description: UI event context bound inside event handler attributes (onclick, onmousemove, onkeydown, etc.). Properties reflect the triggering DOM/UI event. Not available outside event handlers.

alt.description: Whether the Alt/Option key was pressed

target_id.description: id attribute of the event target element

target_class.description: class attribute of the event target element

client_x.description: Cursor X position in client viewport coordinates

client_y.description: Cursor Y position in client viewport coordinates

offset_x.description: Cursor X position relative to the event target

offset_y.description: Cursor Y position relative to the event target

edge.description: Edge identifier for resize events (left, right, top, bottom)

resize_cursor.description: CSS cursor style for the active resize edge

node.description: Tree node context bound per-row during tree_view rendering. Properties describe the element at the current row. Used by the layers panel tree template.

type.description: Element type token (line, rect, group, layer, etc.)

is_container.description: Whether the element is a container (group or layer)

locked.description: Whether the element is locked from editing

element_selected.description: Whether the element is selected on the canvas

panel_selected.description: Whether the element is selected in the layers panel

ancestor_layer_color.description: Color of the nearest ancestor Layer (inherited visual cue)

depth.description: Nesting depth from the document root (0 = top level)

search_ancestor_only.description: True when visible in search results only because a descendant matched

prop.description: Template instantiation context bound when a parameterized template is expanded. Properties come from the instantiation site. Currently used for tab-creation templates.

index.description: Numeric index from the instantiation site (e.g. tab number)

stroke_align.description: Active stroke alignment relative to the path

stroke_dash_1.description: Active stroke dash length for pair 1

stroke_gap_1.description: Active stroke gap length for pair 1

stroke_dash_2.description: Active stroke dash length for pair 2 (null = unused)

stroke_gap_2.description: Active stroke gap length for pair 2 (null = unused)

stroke_dash_3.description: Active stroke dash length for pair 3 (null = unused)

stroke_gap_3.description: Active stroke gap length for pair 3 (null = unused)

stroke_dash_align_anchors.description: When true, dash and gap lengths flex per-segment so a dash is centered on every anchor and a full dash sits at each open path end. When false (default), the dash pattern lays out by exact length along the path. See DASH_ALIGN.md.

stroke_link_arrowhead_scale.description: Active stroke arrowhead scales are linked

stroke_brush.description: Active stroke brush, encoded as "<library_slug>/<brush_slug>". Null when no brush is applied; non-null causes the brush renderer to take over from the native stroke pipeline. See transcripts/BRUSHES.md §Stroke styling interaction.

stroke_brush_overrides.description: Per-instance brush parameter overrides as compact JSON. Polymorphic by brush type; partial dict layered over the master library brush at render time. Edited via the Brush Options dialog in instance_edit mode.

paintbrush_fidelity.description: 5-stop slider position (1-5) for curve-fit tolerance. Maps to fit_error as [0.5, 2.5, 5.0, 7.5, 10.0] pt. Lower tick = more faithful to raw drag; higher tick = smoother output. See PAINTBRUSH_TOOL.md §Fidelity → fit_error mapping.

paintbrush_fill_new_strokes.description: When true, new Paintbrush commits set fill=state.fill_color on the Path element. When false, fill=none. Does not apply to edit-gesture commits (preservation rules).

paintbrush_keep_selected.description: When true, the path committed by a Paintbrush drag becomes the canvas selection after commit.

paintbrush_edit_selected_paths.description: When true, Option/Alt-drag within paintbrush_edit_within px of a selected Path rewrites the nearest sub-segment of that path instead of starting a new stroke. See PAINTBRUSH_TOOL.md §Edit gesture.

paintbrush_edit_within.description: Proximity threshold in px for the Paintbrush edit gesture. Press and release distances are both bounded by this value.

blob_brush_fidelity.description: 5-stop slider position (1-5) for commit-time polygon simplification. Maps to RDP epsilon as [0.5, 2.5, 5.0, 7.5, 10.0] pt. Lower tick = more faithful to raw sweep; higher tick = smoother output boundary. See BLOB_BRUSH_TOOL.md §Fidelity → simplification tolerance mapping.

blob_brush_keep_selected.description: When true, the path committed / merged by a Blob Brush sweep becomes the canvas selection after commit. Default false (Blob Brush is less selection-oriented than Paintbrush, whose equivalent flag defaults true).

blob_brush_merge_only_with_selection.description: When true, a painting-mode sweep merges only with existing Blob Brush elements that are part of the current canvas selection. Independent of Erase mode, which always acts on all blob-brush elements regardless of selection. See BLOB_BRUSH_TOOL.md §Merge condition.

blob_brush_size.description: Default tip diameter in pt. Consulted only when no Calligraphic brush is active; a Calligraphic brush's own size (with overrides + variation) takes precedence. See BLOB_BRUSH_TOOL.md §Runtime tip resolution.

blob_brush_angle.description: Default tip rotation in degrees. Consulted only when no Calligraphic brush is active.

blob_brush_roundness.description: Default tip aspect ratio (percent: 100 = circle; lower = narrower oval). Consulted only when no Calligraphic brush is active.

magic_wand_fill_color.description: When true, Fill Color contributes to the wand match predicate. The criterion compares Euclidean distance in 0–255 sRGB space against magic_wand_fill_tolerance.

magic_wand_fill_tolerance.description: Maximum Euclidean RGB distance (0–255 scale) at which two solid fills are considered similar. Integer 0–255. Default 32 matches the reference design.

magic_wand_stroke_color.description: When true, Stroke Color contributes to the wand match predicate. Same Euclidean / 0–255 sRGB rule as Fill Color against magic_wand_stroke_tolerance.

magic_wand_stroke_tolerance.description: Maximum Euclidean RGB distance (0–255 scale) at which two solid strokes are considered similar. Integer 0–255.

magic_wand_stroke_weight.description: When true, Stroke Weight contributes to the wand match predicate. Compares |candidate.width − seed.width| against magic_wand_stroke_weight_tolerance (in pt).

magic_wand_stroke_weight_tolerance.description: Maximum stroke-weight delta in points at which two strokes are considered similar. Float ≥ 0. Default 5.0.

magic_wand_opacity.description: When true, Opacity contributes to the wand match predicate. Compares |candidate.opacity − seed.opacity| × 100 against magic_wand_opacity_tolerance (in percentage points).

magic_wand_opacity_tolerance.description: Maximum opacity delta in percentage points at which two elements are considered similar. Integer 0–100. The document model stores opacity in [0.0, 1.0]; the predicate multiplies by 100 so the user-facing tolerance is in percentage points.

magic_wand_blending_mode.description: When true, Blending Mode contributes to the wand match predicate. Compares blend_mode enum values for exact equality — there is no tolerance, blend mode is categorical. Default false (the reference design ships this checkbox unchecked).

eyedropper_fill.description: Master toggle for the Fill attribute group.

eyedropper_stroke.description: Master toggle for the Stroke attribute group.

eyedropper_stroke_color.description: When true (and Stroke master is true), copies the source's stroke color (solid / none / gradient / pattern).

eyedropper_stroke_weight.description: When true (and Stroke master is true), copies stroke-width.

eyedropper_stroke_cap_join.description: When true (and Stroke master is true), copies stroke-linecap, stroke-linejoin, and stroke-miterlimit.

eyedropper_stroke_align.description: When true (and Stroke master is true), copies stroke-align (center / inside / outside).

eyedropper_stroke_dash.description: When true (and Stroke master is true), copies the dashed flag and the six dash/gap values.

eyedropper_stroke_arrowheads.description: When true (and Stroke master is true), copies start/end arrowhead shapes, scales, scale-link, and arrow-align.

eyedropper_stroke_profile.description: When true (and Stroke master is true), copies the variable-width profile and its flipped flag.

eyedropper_stroke_brush.description: When true (and Stroke master is true), copies jas:stroke-brush.

eyedropper_opacity.description: Master toggle for the Opacity attribute group.

eyedropper_opacity_alpha.description: When true (and Opacity master is true), copies element.opacity.

eyedropper_opacity_blend.description: When true (and Opacity master is true), copies element.mode (blend mode).

eyedropper_character.description: Master toggle for the Character attribute group. Sub-toggles are no-ops when the source or target isn't a text element.

eyedropper_character_font.description: When true (and Character master is true), copies font family and style (font-weight + font-style).

eyedropper_character_size.description: When true (and Character master is true), copies font size.

eyedropper_character_leading.description: When true (and Character master is true), copies leading.

eyedropper_character_kerning.description: When true (and Character master is true), copies kerning.

eyedropper_character_tracking.description: When true (and Character master is true), copies tracking.

eyedropper_character_color.description: When true (and Character master is true), copies character color.

eyedropper_paragraph.description: Master toggle for the Paragraph attribute group. Sub-toggles are no-ops when the source or target isn't a text element.

eyedropper_paragraph_align.description: When true (and Paragraph master is true), copies paragraph alignment.

eyedropper_paragraph_indent.description: When true (and Paragraph master is true), copies left, right, and first-line indents.

eyedropper_paragraph_space.description: When true (and Paragraph master is true), copies space before and space after.

eyedropper_paragraph_hyphenate.description: When true (and Paragraph master is true), copies the hyphenate flag.

eyedropper_cache.description: Most recently sampled appearance, or null. Plain click on an eligible source writes here; Alt+click reads here to apply to a target. Persists with the document so a non-null cache survives save/load. Cleared by Escape (or replaced by a fresh sample).

transform_reference_point.description: Shared pivot for Scale / Rotate / Shear, stored as [x, y] in absolute document coordinates. null means "use the selection's union bounding-box center." Resets to null on selection change. Custom values are written by a click on canvas while a transform tool is active. Visualised as a cyan-blue crosshair overlay (reference_point_cross render type) when (a) the active tool is one of scale/rotate/shear and (b) a selection exists.

scale_uniform.description: Uniform mode (single percentage) vs Non-Uniform (independent H/V) in the Scale Options dialog

scale_uniform_pct.description: Most-recent uniform-mode scale percentage entered in the Scale Options dialog

scale_horizontal_pct.description: Most-recent non-uniform horizontal percentage entered in the Scale Options dialog

scale_vertical_pct.description: Most-recent non-uniform vertical percentage entered in the Scale Options dialog

scale_strokes.description: When true, scaling multiplies stroke-width by the unsigned geometric mean √(|sx · sy|). When false, stroke-width is preserved. Brush-stroke size overrides scale by the same factor. See SCALE_TOOL.md §Apply behavior.

scale_corners.description: When true, scaling multiplies a rounded_rect's rx by |sx| and ry by |sy|. No-op on other element types. Default false matches the reference dialog. See SCALE_TOOL.md §Apply behavior.

scale_preview.description: When true, the Scale Options dialog drives a live preview overlay (per-element transform-matrix override). No document mutation until OK

rotate_angle.description: Most-recent angle (degrees) entered in the Rotate Options dialog. Positive values rotate counter-clockwise

rotate_preview.description: When true, the Rotate Options dialog drives a live preview overlay

shear_angle.description: Most-recent shear angle (degrees) entered in the Shear Options dialog

shear_axis.description: Shear axis selected in the Shear Options dialog: horizontal (x-axis fixed), vertical (y-axis fixed), or custom (use shear_axis_angle)

shear_axis_angle.description: Custom-axis angle (degrees from horizontal) used when shear_axis == 'custom'

shear_preview.description: When true, the Shear Options dialog drives a live preview overlay

align_to.description: Align-To target mode. 'selection' uses the current selection bounding box; 'artboard' uses the active artboard rectangle; 'key_object' uses a designated element from the selection as the fixed reference.

align_key_object_path.description: Path of the designated key object while align_to == 'key_object'. Null when no key has been designated or while align_to is not in key_object mode.

align_distribute_spacing.description: Explicit gap in points applied by Distribute Spacing operations when align_to == 'key_object' and a key has been designated. Otherwise the two spacing buttons average existing gaps.

align_use_preview_bounds.description: When true, Align / Distribute / Distribute Spacing operations consult each element's preview bounds (stroke-inclusive); otherwise they consult geometric bounds.

last_boolean_op.description: Most-recently-applied boolean operation. Populated by the destructive and compound-creating actions; consumed by "Repeat Boolean Operation". Make / Release / Expand Compound Shape do not write this field.

boolean_precision.description: Geometric tolerance in points for Bezier flattening, Bezier refit, and redundant-point removal. Edited via the Boolean Options dialog. Default 0.0283 pt = 0.01 mm.

boolean_remove_redundant_points.description: When true, collinear points in boolean-op output are collapsed within Precision after each operation. Edited via the Boolean Options dialog.

boolean_divide_remove_unpainted.description: When true, DIVIDE fragments with no fill and no stroke are discarded rather than kept as invisible paths. Edited via the Boolean Options dialog.

brushes_selected_library.description: Library slug owning the current panel selection. Changing libraries clears brushes_selected_brushes.

brushes_selected_brushes.description: List of brush slugs selected within brushes_selected_library. Slug-keyed (not index-keyed) so library reordering or duplication does not scramble the selection.

brushes_open_libraries.description: Libraries currently open as disclosure sections in the panel body, each with its collapsed state. Initialised at startup from preferences.brushes.persistent_libraries; falls back to [{ id: default_brushes, collapsed: false }] when the preference list is empty.

brushes_thumbnail_size.description: Brushes panel thumbnail tile size. Small=48x16, Medium=72x24, Large=96x32. Disabled (radio dim) when view_mode == list.

brushes_category_filter.description: Brush types currently visible in the panel body. Each toggled independently from the panel menu's "Show X Brushes" checkbox group. Empty list renders the body with a "No brushes match current filter" hint.

fill_on_top.description: Whether the fill swatch renders in front of the stroke swatch

toolbar_visible.description: Whether the toolbar pane is visible

canvas_visible.description: Whether the canvas pane is visible

dock_visible.description: Whether the dock panel pane is visible

canvas_maximized.description: Whether the canvas fills the viewport

dock_collapsed.description: Whether the dock is in collapsed (chevron-only) state

active_tab.description: Index of the active document tab (-1 = none)

_drag_pane.description: Internal: element id of the pane currently being dragged

_drag_offset_x.description: Internal: cursor X offset from pane origin at drag start

_drag_offset_y.description: Internal: cursor Y offset from pane origin at drag start

_resize_pane.description: Internal: element id of the pane currently being resized

_resize_edge.description: Internal: which edge is being resized (left, right, top, bottom)

_resize_start_x.description: Internal: cursor X position at resize start

_resize_start_y.description: Internal: cursor Y position at resize start

align_panel_content.description:
Align and distribute operations on the current selection. The panel performs three families of operations:
- Alignment: move elements so their edges or centers coincide
  along a chosen axis.
- Distribute: move elements so a chosen edge or center is
  evenly spaced along an axis.
- Distribute Spacing: move elements so the gaps between them
  are uniform along an axis.

All three families share a single Align To target: selection, artboard, or a designated key object from the selection. The Use Preview Bounds panel-menu toggle selects whether operations consult geometric or preview (stroke-inclusive) bounding boxes.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 — "Align Objects:" label. Row 2 — six icon buttons: left, horizontal-center, right
  (horizontal-axis alignment) on the left half; top, vertical-
  center, bottom (vertical-axis alignment) on the right half.
Row 3 — "Distribute Objects:" label. Row 4 — six icon buttons: distribute-left, distribute-
  horizontal-center, distribute-right on the left half;
  distribute-top, distribute-vertical-center, distribute-
  bottom on the right half.
Row 5 — two halves. Left half: "Distribute Spacing:" label,
  two spacing icon buttons (vertical, horizontal), and a pt
  value input. Right half: "Align To:" label and three
  mutually-exclusive icon_button toggles (artboard, selection,
  key-object).

Action buttons behave as one-shot operations — clicking fires the alignment and never leaves a persistent checked state. Align To toggles form a radio group and are checked when panel.align_to matches their target. Per ALIGN.md §Widget types.
Enable / disable rules: - Align operations require at least 2 elements selected. - Distribute and Distribute Spacing operations require at
  least 3 elements selected.
- When align_to == "key_object" and no key has been designated,
  every operation button disables.
- DISTRIBUTE_SPACING_VALUE enables only when align_to ==
  "key_object" and a key has been designated.

align_to.description: Align-To target mode. Mirrors state.align_to. Per ALIGN.md §Align To target.

key_object_path.description: Path of the designated key object while align_to == 'key_object'. Null when no key is designated.

distribute_spacing_value.description: Explicit gap in points for Distribute Spacing operations when a key object is designated. Otherwise unused.

use_preview_bounds.description: When true, operations consult preview (stroke-inclusive) bounds; otherwise geometric bounds.

children.description: ALIGN_LEFT_BUTTON. Move elements so their left edges coincide along the horizontal axis.
ALIGN_HORIZONTAL_CENTER_BUTTON. Move elements so their horizontal centers coincide.
ALIGN_RIGHT_BUTTON. Move elements so their right edges coincide along the horizontal axis.
ALIGN_TOP_BUTTON. Move elements so their top edges coincide along the vertical axis.
ALIGN_VERTICAL_CENTER_BUTTON. Move elements so their vertical centers coincide.
ALIGN_BOTTOM_BUTTON. Move elements so their bottom edges coincide along the vertical axis.
DISTRIBUTE_LEFT_BUTTON. Redistribute elements horizontally so the left coordinates of their bounding boxes are evenly spaced.
DISTRIBUTE_HORIZONTAL_CENTER_BUTTON. Redistribute elements horizontally so their horizontal centers are evenly spaced.
DISTRIBUTE_RIGHT_BUTTON. Redistribute elements horizontally so the right coordinates of their bounding boxes are evenly spaced.
DISTRIBUTE_TOP_BUTTON. Redistribute elements vertically so the top coordinates of their bounding boxes are evenly spaced.
DISTRIBUTE_VERTICAL_CENTER_BUTTON. Redistribute elements vertically so their vertical centers are evenly spaced.
DISTRIBUTE_BOTTOM_BUTTON. Redistribute elements vertically so the bottom coordinates of their bounding boxes are evenly spaced.
DISTRIBUTE_VERTICAL_SPACING_BUTTON. Move elements vertically so gaps between them are uniform.
DISTRIBUTE_HORIZONTAL_SPACING_BUTTON. Move elements horizontally so gaps between them are uniform.
DISTRIBUTE_SPACING_VALUE. Explicit gap in points for Distribute Spacing when Align To is Key Object and a key has been designated. Range 0–1296 pt.
ALIGN_TO_ARTBOARD_BUTTON. Use the active artboard rectangle as the reference for every Align and Distribute operation.
ALIGN_TO_SELECTION_BUTTON. Use the current selection bounding box as the reference. This is the default mode.
ALIGN_TO_KEY_OBJECT_BUTTON. Use a designated element from the selection as the fixed reference. The key is designated by clicking an already-selected element on the canvas.

artboards_panel_content.description:
Artboards panel — every document has at least one artboard (print page region) with a unique internal id, a positional 1-based number, a user-editable name, position/size, and a fill. New documents seed a single 612×792pt (US Letter) artboard; subsequent New Artboard actions inherit size from the topmost existing artboard. See transcripts/ARTBOARDS.md for the full specification.
=== Row Layout ===
Each row is laid out left to right as:
ARTBOARD_NUMBER — ARTBOARD_NAME — ARTBOARD_OPTIONS_BUTTON
ARTBOARD_NUMBER is the 1-based position of the row in the list. It is a derived value, not a stored identifier. Delete, reorder, and undo all renumber automatically so the column is always dense (1..N with no gaps). Clicking the number cell makes that row the sole panel-selected row — a shortcut for "make current".
ARTBOARD_NAME is the artboard's user-editable name. Click selects the row using standard click / shift-click / cmd-click rules. Click-and-wait on the name, F2, Enter, or the Rename menu entry enters inline rename mode; Enter or blur commits, Escape cancels. Trim-on-commit strips whitespace; empty names silently revert. Duplicate names are allowed.
ARTBOARD_OPTIONS_BUTTON opens the Artboard Options Dialogue for that row's artboard regardless of panel-selection. No selection side effects.
=== Panel Selection ===
Panel-selection is a set of 0..N artboards tracked by stable id. Click replaces the selection with that row; shift-click extends from the anchor to the clicked row; cmd-click toggles the clicked row in or out of the selection. Clicking empty panel area (below the last row) is a no-op. Panel-selection survives reorder because it keys on id, not position.
=== Current Artboard ===
Current is exactly one artboard, always. Derived as `topmost panel-selected artboard, else artboard at position 1`. Used by ALIGN_TO_ARTBOARD_BUTTON and any future fit-to-artboard / zoom-to-artboard commands. There is no canvas indicator for current — it's a tiebreaker for commands, not a UI state.
=== Reordering ===
Drag panel-selected rows to a new position, use MOVE_UP_BUTTON / MOVE_DOWN_BUTTON in the footer, or press Option+Up / Option+Down. For discontiguous selections the swap-with-neighbor-skipping- selected-neighbors rule applies: iterate top-to-bottom; each selected row swaps with the row above it, skipping rows that are themselves selected or already at position 1. Move Down is symmetric. Each reorder is a single undo op.
=== Footer ===
REARRANGE_BUTTON sits at the bottom-left; the remaining buttons cluster at the bottom-right in order MOVE_UP_BUTTON, MOVE_DOWN_ BUTTON, NEW_ARTBOARD_BUTTON, DELETE_ARTBOARD_BUTTON.
REARRANGE_BUTTON is phase-1 deferred; it opens the Rearrange Dialogue which does not exist yet. An accent dot appears on the button when the artboards list has changed since the Dialogue was last opened; the dot clears on Dialogue open. Because the Dialogue is deferred, the dot lights on first list change and remains lit.
DELETE_ARTBOARD_BUTTON is grayed when deletion would violate the at-least-one-artboard invariant (see below).
NEW_ARTBOARD_BUTTON creates silently (no Dialogue). Default size inherits from the topmost existing artboard, else 612×792 pt. Default position offset `(20, 20)` pt from the topmost panel- selected artboard's top-left, else `(0, 0)`. All display toggles off, transparent fill, name `Artboard N` with smallest unused N.
=== At-Least-One-Artboard Invariant ===
`document.artboards.length >= 1` is enforced at every observable state. `Delete Artboards` (menu, footer, Delete / Backspace) is grayed when panel-selection spans all existing artboards. Load- time repair inserts a default artboard when the document has `artboards: []` or no `artboards:` key at all, logging `Document had no artboards; inserted default.`
=== Phase-1 Deferrals ===
Artboard Tool (canvas-side create / move / resize), Convert to Artboards (menu grayed), Rearrange Dialogue (menu + footer button grayed), `update_while_dragging` behavior, `video_ruler_pixel_aspect_ratio` visual effect, and all printing. Values for deferred stored fields persist and round-trip.

artboards_panel_selection.description: List of artboard ids that are panel-selected

panel_selection_anchor.description: Artboard id that anchors shift-click range selection

renaming_artboard.description: Id of the artboard currently being renamed inline, or null

reference_point.description: Which of the 9 anchor points the X / Y inputs in the Artboard Options Dialogue display and accept. Underlying storage is always the top-left corner; this widget controls display and entry only. Persists as a panel preference, not per document.

rearrange_dirty.description: True when the artboards list has changed since the Rearrange Dialogue was last opened. Surfaces as the blue-dot accent on REARRANGE_BUTTON. Phase-1: once set, never cleared (Dialogue is deferred).

boolean_panel_content.description:
The Boolean panel performs boolean operations on the geometry of the current selection. Nine destructive operations and four compound- shape variants let the user combine overlapping paths into unified shapes or live (non-destructive) compound shapes.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 — "Shape Modes:" label. Row 2 — four icon buttons (UNION, SUBTRACT_FRONT, INTERSECTION,
  EXCLUDE) on the left half; an "Expand" button on the right half
  that flattens the selected compound shape(s) to static paths.
Row 3 — five icon buttons (DIVIDE, TRIM, MERGE, CROP,
  SUBTRACT_BACK). A sixth slot is reserved for OUTLINE, which is
  deferred until the planar-graph / DCEL primitive ships.

Alt/Option-clicking UNION, SUBTRACT_FRONT, INTERSECTION, or EXCLUDE creates a live compound shape that re-evaluates when its operands are edited. A plain click applies the boolean destructively.
Enable / disable rules: - Every operation button requires at least 2 selected elements. - The Expand button and Release / Expand Compound Shape menu items
  require the selection to contain at least one compound shape.
- Repeat Boolean Operation enables only when last_operation is not
  null and the current selection satisfies that op's gate.

See `transcripts/BOOLEAN.md` for the complete spec, operand and paint rules, Boolean Options dialog, and cross-language parity expectations.

last_operation.description: Most-recently-applied operation. Populated by each op action; consumed by "Repeat Boolean Operation". Mirrors state.last_boolean_op.

children.description: UNION_BUTTON. Merge all selected elements into a single element taking the union of their geometries. Result carries the frontmost operand's paint. Alt/Option+click creates a live compound shape instead.

SUBTRACT_FRONT_BUTTON. Subtract the frontmost element from all others in the selection. The frontmost is consumed; remaining elements keep their own paint. Alt/Option+click creates a live compound shape instead.
INTERSECTION_BUTTON. Keep only the region covered by every selected element. Result carries the frontmost operand's paint. Alt/Option+click creates a live compound shape instead.
EXCLUDE_BUTTON. Keep the region covered by exactly one selected element (symmetric difference). Result carries the frontmost operand's paint. Alt/Option+click creates a live compound shape instead.

EXPAND_BUTTON. Flatten every selected compound shape into static Polygon elements derived from its evaluated geometry. The compound shape's paint is carried to each expanded polygon; the operand tree is discarded. Enabled only when the selection contains at least one compound shape.
DIVIDE_BUTTON. Cut the elements apart so none overlap. Each fragment inherits the paint of the frontmost original element that covered it.
TRIM_BUTTON. Remove parts of elements that are hidden behind other elements. Each survivor keeps its own paint.
MERGE_BUTTON. TRIM first, then union any touching survivors whose fills are exactly equal.
CROP_BUTTON. Use the frontmost element as a mask and crop all others to its interior. The mask is consumed; each cropped survivor keeps its own paint.
SUBTRACT_BACK_BUTTON. Subtract the backmost element from all others. The backmost is consumed; remaining elements keep their own paint.

brushes_panel_content.description:
Brush library panel for managing named brushes organised into libraries. The panel displays a scrollable area of collapsible library sections (each rendering its brushes as tiles) above a bottom toolbar with five icon buttons.
Phase 1 ships Calligraphic brushes only. Scatter, Art, Pattern, and Bristle types follow as their data models and renderers land. See transcripts/BRUSHES.md.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 — Brush tiles area. Scrollable container rendering one collapsible disclosure section per entry in panel.open_libraries. Each section header shows the library name; expanded sections render the library's brushes as tiles (1:3 aspect at small size, matching BRUSHES.md §Controls).
Row 2 — Bottom toolbar (left to right): Brush Libraries menu, Remove Brush Stroke, Brush Options for Selection, New Brush, Delete Brush.
Single-click a tile to select it AND set state.stroke_brush AND apply jas:stroke-brush to the canvas selection. Selection uses the generic select effect with mode=auto: plain click, Shift+click for ranges, Cmd/Ctrl+click to toggle. Selection is per-library; the selected_brushes panel state tracks slugs (not indices) within selected_library.
Double-click a tile to open the Brush Options dialog in library_edit mode for the master brush.

selected_library.description: Library slug owning the current panel selection.

selected_brushes.description: Brush slugs selected within selected_library. Slug-keyed so library reordering or duplication does not scramble the selection.

open_libraries.description: List of open libraries with their collapsed state. Each entry has an "id" (library slug) and "collapsed" (bool).

thumbnail_size.description: Thumbnail tile size when view_mode == thumbnail. small=48x16, medium=72x24, large=96x32.

category_filter.description: Brush types currently visible. Toggled per-type from the panel menu's "Show X Brushes" checkboxes.

menu.label: New Brush
menu.description: Open the Brush Options dialog in create mode with the canvas selection's geometry pre-filled as the new brush's artwork. OK appends a new brush to the selected library.

menu.label: Duplicate Brush
menu.description: Create a copy of each currently selected brush in the selected library. The copies are inserted immediately after their originals with " copy" appended to the name and a fresh slug. The copies become the new selection.

menu.label: Delete Brush
menu.description: Remove the currently selected brushes from the selected library. Any element with jas:stroke-brush referencing a deleted brush re-renders as a plain stroke (null-on-missing fallback). Undoable.

menu.label: Brush Options...
menu.description: Open the Brush Options dialog in library_edit mode for the first selected brush. OK prompts the Apply to Strokes confirm dialog.

menu.label: Select All Unused
menu.description: Replace selected_brushes with every brush in the panel whose slug does not appear as jas:stroke-brush on any element in the document.

menu.label: Sort by Name
menu.description: Permanently reorder the selected library's brushes alphabetically (case-sensitive, lexicographic). Slugs unchanged; selection preserved.

menu.label: Thumbnail View
menu.description: Render each tile as a stroke preview thumbnail.

menu.label: List View
menu.description: Render each tile as an icon + name row.

menu.label: Small Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display brush thumbnails at 48x16 pixels.

menu.label: Medium Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display brush thumbnails at 72x24 pixels.

menu.label: Large Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display brush thumbnails at 96x32 pixels.

menu.label: Show Calligraphic Brushes
menu.description: Toggle visibility of Calligraphic brushes in the panel body.

menu.label: Show Scatter Brushes
menu.description: Toggle visibility of Scatter brushes.

menu.label: Show Art Brushes
menu.description: Toggle visibility of Art brushes.

menu.label: Show Pattern Brushes
menu.description: Toggle visibility of Pattern brushes.

menu.label: Show Bristle Brushes
menu.description: Toggle visibility of Bristle brushes.

menu.label: Make Persistent
menu.description: Toggle the selected library's presence in the user's persistent_libraries preference. Persistent libraries auto-open on app launch.

menu.label: Open Brush Library
menu.description: Submenu listing all brush libraries discovered under workspace/brushes/<slug>/library.json. Selecting a library opens or closes it (toggle via checkmark).

menu.label: Save Brush Library
menu.description: Open the Save Brush Library dialog. The selected library's manifest and artwork files are written to workspace/brushes/<new_slug>/.

children.description:
Scrollable area of collapsible library sections. Each section is a disclosure (triangle + name); expanding renders the library's brushes as tiles. Tile size and view mode are controlled by panel.thumbnail_size / panel.view_mode.
Single-click a tile to select it, set state.stroke_brush, and apply jas:stroke-brush to the canvas selection. Double-click opens Brush Options in library_edit mode.
Opens the Open Brush Library dynamic submenu listing all available libraries.
Strip jas:stroke-brush and jas:stroke-brush-overrides from every selected element; null state.stroke_brush.
Open the Brush Options dialog in instance_edit mode for the selected stroke. Edits the per-instance overrides.
Open the Brush Options dialog in create mode with the canvas selection pre-filled as the new brush's artwork.
Permanently remove every brush in selected_brushes from the selected library. Undoable.

character_panel_content.description:
Character formatting panel — initial placeholder implementation. Provides font family, style-name, font size, leading, kerning, tracking, vertical and horizontal scale, baseline shift, character rotation, six character toggle buttons (All Caps, Small Caps, Superscript, Subscript, Underline, Strikethrough), language, and anti-aliasing mode, all bound to panel-local state.
Everything here is a placeholder bound to panel-local state — no control is yet wired to per-tspan attributes on the selection. Real editing arrives alongside the tspan-aware Character panel work (set_character_attribute via the unified TSPAN.md algorithm, mixed-value display for character-range selections, parens-default display for computed defaults, and the Snap to Glyph section) once that plumbing is in place across all four native apps.

style_name.description: Style name, e.g. Regular, Italic, Bold, Bold Italic.

leading.description: Leading (line height) in points. Default is the Auto value (120% of font size, 14.4 for 12pt). Full spec uses a parens-wrapped display for the Auto case.

kerning.description: Kerning. Named modes Auto / Optical / Metrics pass through to the element's kerning attribute verbatim. Numeric values are entered in 1/1000 em and serialise to the element as "{N}em". Empty, "0", or "Auto" round-trip to an empty element attribute (the default).

baseline_shift.description: Baseline shift in points, signed; positive = up.

character_rotation.description: Character rotation in degrees, signed; positive = clockwise (SVG convention).

all_caps.description: All Caps. Mutually exclusive with Small Caps in the full spec; not enforced yet.

superscript.description: Superscript. Mutually exclusive with Subscript in the full spec; not enforced yet.

anti_aliasing.description: Anti-aliasing mode: None, Sharp, Crisp, Strong, or Smooth.

snap_to_glyph_visible.description: Whether the Snap to Glyph section is visible in the panel.

snap_baseline.description: Enable snapping to glyph baselines on the canvas.

snap_glyph_bounds.description: Enable snapping to glyph bounding-box edges.

snap_proximity_guides.description: Enable snapping to glyph proximity guides.

snap_angular_guides.description: Enable snapping to glyph slant lines.

snap_anchor_point.description: Enable snapping to glyph anchor points.

children.description: Font family. Typing filters the list (typeahead).
Verbatim style name from the font (e.g. Regular, Italic, Bold, Bold Italic).
Font size in points.
Line leading in points; Auto = 120% of font size.

Kerning between characters. Named modes (Auto, Optical, Metrics) pass through to the element's kerning attribute verbatim. Free numeric entry is treated as 1/1000 em and serialises to "{N}em". Named-mode text is preserved in the panel and on the element.
Uniform letter-spacing in 1/1000 em, signed.
Vertical scale in percent (1–10000).
Horizontal scale in percent (1–10000).
Baseline shift in points; positive = up.
Per-glyph rotation in degrees; positive = clockwise (SVG convention).
Render the selection in uppercase.
Render lowercase letters as x-height-sized capitals.
Position the selection above the baseline.
Position the selection below the baseline.
Underline the selected text.
Strike through the selected text.

Language of the selected text, stored as an ISO 639-1 code on the `xml:lang` attribute (used for hyphenation and line-breaking). Dropdown options show the language name; the value stored on the element is the code.
Anti-aliasing mode: None, Sharp, Crisp, Strong, or Smooth.
Snap to the invisible baseline letters sit on.
Snap to the top of lowercase letters (x, a, e, ...).
Snap to the left/right/top/bottom edges of a specific letter.
Snap to guides near the Baseline, x-Height, and Glyph Bounds, based on the glyph's shape and layout.
Snap to the slant lines of letters like V or A.
Snap to the actual mathematical anchor points on glyph outlines.

color_panel_content.description:
Inline color panel for editing the active fill or stroke color (whichever swatch is in front, per fill_on_top). The panel has five color modes (Grayscale, RGB, HSB, CMYK, Web Safe RGB) stored as panel-local state. Changing the mode shows the appropriate slider set without changing the current color. The mode is re-initialized from the active color when the panel first opens.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 — Swatches. Three fixed shortcuts (None, Black, White) followed by a thin vertical rule and ten recent-color slots. Recent colors are per-document and shared between fill and stroke. Colors are added to the front of the list on commit (slider release, hex Enter, or swatch click). Duplicates move to front rather than duplicating. Empty slots render as hollow squares with a solid border.
Row 2 — Controls (two columns). Left column: the standard fill/stroke widget (overlapping swatches + swap + reset buttons), same visual behavior as the toolbar widget except single-click only (no double-click to open the modal picker). Right column: sliders for the active color mode.
Row 3 — Hex field. A six-character hex input (RRGGBB, displayed with a leading # label). Committing updates the active color and adds it to recent colors. In Web Safe RGB mode, the value snaps to the nearest web-safe color.
Bottom: a 64 px tall color gradient (color bar). Hue varies along the x-axis (0–360°). The y-axis is split into two halves: in the top half, saturation increases from 0% to 100% while brightness decreases from 100% to 80%; in the bottom half, saturation stays at 100% while brightness decreases from 80% to 0%. This produces a gradient that transitions from white/pastel at the top through fully saturated colors in the middle to black at the bottom. Clicking or dragging sets the active color, committing to the recent-colors list on pointer-up.
Sliders and the hex field are disabled (non-interactive) when the active attribute is none. Clicking any swatch (fixed or recent) implicitly un-nones the attribute and commits the swatch color.
Panel menu: five color-mode entries (with a checkmark on the active mode), Invert (255−R, 255−G, 255−B), Complement ((H+180) mod 360, S, B — no-op if S=0), and a permanently disabled Create New Swatch… entry.

hex.description: Working hex string (6 chars, no # prefix)

recent_colors.description: Per-document list of recently committed colors, newest first. Max 10 entries. A duplicate color moves to front rather than duplicating. Added on commit: slider release, hex field Enter, swatch click (including none/black/white). Not added for Invert or Complement — those dispatch set_active_color which adds there. Empty slots render as hollow squares with a solid border.

gradient_panel_content.description:
Gradient panel — edits the gradient value of the active fill or stroke on the current selection. See transcripts/GRADIENT.md for the full specification.
Phase 2 scope: yaml wiring + static layout only. Controls render at the positions specified in §Layout but do not yet write back to the document. Phase 4 wires panel reads; Phase 5 wires panel writes and fill-type coupling. Linear/radial render natively in SVG (Phase 6/7); freeform, smooth method, dither, and along/across stroke sub-modes are flagged status: pending_renderer.
=== Layout ===
Row 1 — "Presets:" label, DOCUMENT_LIBRARY_DROPDOWN (selects which library populates the tile strip), DOCUMENT_LIBRARY_SIZE_DROPDOWN (Small / Medium / Large Thumbnail View, plus Small / Large List View flagged pending_renderer).
Row 2 — Gradient tile strip; one GRADIENT_TILE per gradient in the active library.
Row 3 — Horizontal rule separator.
Row 4 — Two-column layout: FILL_STROKE_WIDGET on the left (col-3), middle block on the right (col-9) containing Type / Stroke sub-mode / Angle / Aspect ratio / Method / Dither rows.
Row 5 — Stops editor: ADD_TO_SWATCHES_BUTTON (col-2), GRADIENT_SLIDER (col-8), TRASH_BUTTON (col-2).
Row 6 — Selected-stop properties: EYEDROPPER_BUTTON (col-2), inner column with Opacity / Location combos.
=== Panel menu ===
Six entries across three groups: Reverse Gradient / Distribute Stops Evenly / Reset Midpoints (gradient transformations); Open Gradient Library / Save Gradient Library (library management); Close Gradient (panel management).

type.description: Working gradient type. Phase-1 is panel-local; Phase 5 persists to the active gradient on selection.

angle.description: Working gradient angle in degrees, -180..+180. Linear/radial only.

aspect_ratio.description: Working aspect ratio as a percentage, 1-1000. 100 = isotropic (circle for radial). Linear/radial only.

method.description: Working interpolation/topology method. classic / smooth apply to linear/radial; points / lines apply to freeform. Smooth and freeform topologies are pending_renderer.

dither.description: When true, the renderer applies sub-pixel dithering to reduce visible banding. status: pending_renderer in all four apps.

stroke_sub_mode.description: How a stroke gradient maps onto the path. along/across are pending_renderer; within renders natively.

stops.description: Working stops for linear/radial. Phase-1 is panel-local; Phase 5 persists to the active gradient on the selection.

selected_stop_index.description: Index of the selected stop in `stops`. -1 when nothing selected. Shared with the on-canvas Gradient tool (see GRADIENT_TOOL.md).

selected_midpoint_index.description: Index of the selected midpoint (the midpoint between stops [i] and [i+1]). -1 when nothing selected. Mutually exclusive with selected_stop_index.

active_library_id.description: The currently-selected library id (filename stem). Defaults to one of the bundled seed libraries so the tile strip is non-empty when the panel opens. Phase 9 wires the dynamic list of all discovered libraries.

thumbnail_size.description: Tile size for the preset strip. Three thumbnail sizes ship in v1; the two list-view items in DOCUMENT_LIBRARY_SIZE_DROPDOWN are status: pending_renderer.

preview_state.description: True when the active attribute (fill or stroke per state.fill_on_top) is solid or none and the panel is showing a seeded default gradient. Cleared on first edit (which promotes the attribute to a gradient — see Phase 5 fill-type coupling).

menu.label: Reverse Gradient
menu.description: Phase 5 — flips stops end-to-end. Disabled until the action pipeline is wired.

menu.label: Open Gradient Library
menu.description: Submenu listing all available gradient libraries discovered from workspace/gradients/*.json files. Selecting a library switches the active library in DOCUMENT_LIBRARY_DROPDOWN so its tiles populate the preset strip. Mirrors the Swatches panel Open Swatch Library submenu behavior.

menu.label: Save Gradient Library
menu.description: Phase 9 — opens a Save Gradient Library dialog. Deferred pending a Save dialog and file-writing pipeline per app.

children.description: Single-select. Phase 3 ships three seed libraries; Phase 9 wires a dynamic list discovered from workspace/gradients/*.json plus the per-document Document Library.

layers_panel_content.description:
Layer hierarchy panel displaying all elements in the document as an interactive tree. Each element occupies one row. The panel supports two independent selection models (element selection and panel selection), drag-and-drop reordering, inline renaming, search and filter, isolation mode, and a Layer Options dialog.
=== Row Layout ===
Each row is laid out left to right as:
SPACER — EYE_BUTTON — LOCK_BUTTON — TWIRL_DOWN_BUTTON — ELEMENT_PREVIEW — ELEMENT_NAME — SELECT_SQUARE
SPACER provides indentation in increments of 16px. Top-level layers are not indented. Each nesting level adds 16px: direct children of a layer are indented 16px, grandchildren 32px, and so on.
EYE_BUTTON displays and controls the element's visibility state (see Visibility below).
LOCK_BUTTON displays and controls whether the element is locked (see Locking below).
TWIRL_DOWN_BUTTON is present on layers and groups; clicking it expands or collapses the container's children in the tree (see Twirl-Down below). Leaf elements (paths, text, images, etc.) do not have a twirl button; a blank gap of the same width preserves column alignment.
ELEMENT_PREVIEW is a 32px square rasterized thumbnail of the element scaled to fit, rendered on a white background. Thumbnails are refreshed as elements change on a best-effort basis. Elements with outline or invisible visibility modes are rendered as if in preview mode for the thumbnail. Empty groups and layers show a blank preview.
ELEMENT_NAME is the name of the element, if it has one. Unnamed elements display their element type in angle brackets, truncated with ellipsis if the name exceeds the available space. The display names are: <Line>, <Rectangle>, <Circle>, <Ellipse>, <Polyline>, <Polygon>, <Path>, <Text>, <Text Path>, <Group>, <Layer>.
SELECT_SQUARE is a 12px square. It is filled with the color of the element's nearest ancestor layer if the element is currently element-selected (see Element Selection below). Otherwise it is empty (transparent fill with a thin border).
=== Visibility ===
Each element has one of three visibility states: preview (normal rendering), outline (wireframe rendering), or invisible (not rendered).
Clicking the EYE_BUTTON cycles visibility: preview -> outline -> invisible -> preview. The icon changes to match: eye_preview (open eye), eye_outline (eye with dashed pupil), or eye_invisible (closed/struck-through eye).
When a container (layer or group) changes visibility, the change applies to all its descendants.
Option-click (Alt-click) on the EYE_BUTTON triggers solo/unsolo among the clicked element's siblings at the same tree level. On the first Option-click, the clicked element becomes the only visible sibling; all other siblings are set to invisible and their previous visibility states are saved. On a second Option-click of the same element, all siblings are restored to their saved visibility states. If the user manually changes any sibling's visibility between the two Option-clicks, the saved state is discarded (no restore).
=== Locking ===
Clicking the LOCK_BUTTON toggles the element's lock state. Locked elements cannot be selected or edited on the canvas but remain visible. The icon shows lock_locked when locked, lock_unlocked when unlocked.
When locking a container, the container saves each direct child's current lock state, then sets all children to locked. When unlocking a container, each direct child's lock state is restored to the value saved at the time the container was locked. This is per-container: each container remembers only its own direct children's states.
Locked elements can still be dragged and reparented in the layers panel (lock protects content editing, not organizational position).
=== Twirl-Down ===
Layers and groups have a TWIRL_DOWN_BUTTON, expanded (open) by default. The icon shows twirl_open when expanded and twirl_closed when collapsed. Twirl state persists across sessions. Leaf elements do not have a twirl button; a blank gap preserves alignment. Empty layers and groups still show an active twirl button.
=== Element Selection ===
Element selection is the document-level selection, synchronized with the canvas. Clicking on the SELECT_SQUARE selects that element and deselects all others. Shift-click extends the selection in visual (flattened) row order from the last-clicked element to the clicked element. Selecting a collapsed group includes all its descendants. Command-click toggles the clicked element in or out of the current selection. Elements can be selected across layers.
When an element is selected on the canvas, the panel auto-scrolls to ensure that element's row is visible. If the element is inside a collapsed group, the group and all ancestor groups are auto-expanded first. If multiple elements are selected, the panel scrolls to the first selected element in document order.
=== Panel Selection ===
Panel selection is independent from element selection and is local to the layers panel. An element is panel-selected by clicking on its spacer, twirl-down gap, preview, or name area. Panel-selected elements are highlighted with the system selection color as a background on the row. Click selects one element and deselects all others. Shift-click extends the selection in visual row order; selecting a collapsed group includes all its descendants. Command-click toggles the element. In isolation mode, panel selection is constrained to the isolated subtree.
Panel-selected elements can be moved within the panel by dragging (see Drag-and-Drop below). All menu operations that refer to "selected" items act on panel selection, not element selection.
Right-clicking a row opens the context menu. If the right-clicked element is not already panel-selected, it becomes the sole panel-selected element first, then the menu acts on the selection.
=== Drag-and-Drop ===
Panel-selected elements can be dragged to reorder or reparent elements in the document. A horizontal line between rows indicates the drop insertion point.
Constraints: - Layers can be dragged to the top level or into other layers.
  Layers cannot be dragged into groups.
- Groups can be dragged into layers and other groups. - Leaf elements can be dragged into layers and groups. - Nothing can be dragged into a locked container. - Nothing can be dragged into itself or its own descendants. - Every element must live inside a layer; elements cannot be dragged
  to the root level unless they are layers.
- When dragging multiple panel-selected elements, all constraints
  must be satisfied for every element. If any element violates a
  constraint, the entire drop is rejected.
- Locked elements can be dragged (lock protects content, not
  position).

Hovering over a collapsed container for 500ms auto-expands it. Elements preserve their relative order when moved. Drag-and-drop is disabled while a search query is active.
=== Search and Filter ===
A search field appears at the top of the panel. Typing in the field incrementally filters the tree to show elements whose name (or auto-generated type name like <Path>) contains the search string, case-insensitive. Ancestor nodes of matching elements are shown to preserve tree context but are dimmed to distinguish them from actual matches. Clearing the field restores the full tree.
A filter button to the right of the search field opens a dropdown with checkboxes for each element type: Layer, Group, Path, Rectangle, Circle, Ellipse, Polyline, Polygon, Text, Text Path, Line. By default all types are checked. Unchecking a type hides all elements of that type. Filtering composes with the search field — both must match for an element to appear.
Collapsed nodes are searched; if a match is inside a collapsed group, the group is auto-expanded to reveal it. Panel selection and element selection are constrained to visible (matching) rows only. In isolation mode, search and filter operate within the isolated subtree only.
=== Isolation Mode ===
Isolation mode restricts editing to a single container and its descendants. Enter isolation by double-clicking a layer or group row (outside the name text area), or via the menu/context menu "Enter Isolation Mode" (enabled when exactly one container is panel-selected).
All content outside the isolated container is dimmed to 10% opacity and becomes non-interactive (cannot be selected, edited, or targeted). The dimmed content remains visible as spatial context.
Isolation is a stack. While in isolation, double-clicking a nested group enters a second level. The outer container also dims, leaving only the inner group's children fully interactive.
A breadcrumb bar appears at the top of the panel showing the isolation path (e.g., "Layer 1 > Group > Inner Group"). Each breadcrumb segment is clickable — clicking a segment exits isolation to that level. The root segment exits isolation entirely.
Exiting isolation: - Escape pops one level. At the outermost level, exits entirely. - Double-click an empty area of the canvas exits one level. - Menu/context menu "Exit Isolation Mode" exits one level. - Click a breadcrumb segment exits to that level directly.
On entering each isolation level, the visibility state of every element is saved. On exit, each element's visibility is restored. If the user changes a visibility state while in isolation, that change is preserved on exit (overwrites the saved value for that element).
Constraints during isolation: - Element selection constrained to the isolated container's
  descendants.
- Panel selection constrained to the isolated subtree. - Drag-and-drop constrained to within the isolated subtree. - "New Layer" and "Collect in New Layer" are disabled. - "New Group" operates within the isolated container.
=== Keyboard Shortcuts ===
- Delete / Backspace: delete panel-selected elements. - F2 or Enter: start renaming the panel-selected element (single
  selection only).
- Command-A: select all visible elements in the panel. - Up arrow: move focus to the previous visible row. - Down arrow: move focus to the next visible row. - Right arrow: expand a collapsed container, or move to its first
  child if already expanded.
- Left arrow: collapse an expanded container, or move to the parent
  if already collapsed or a leaf.
- Escape: exit one level of isolation mode, or cancel an active
  rename.

=== Renaming ===
Renaming is triggered by F2, Enter (when exactly one element is panel-selected), or double-clicking the element name text. The name becomes an editable text field in place. Confirm by pressing Enter or clicking outside the field. Cancel by pressing Escape (reverts to the value before editing). Confirming with an empty string removes the custom name — the element reverts to its auto-generated type name. Duplicate names are allowed. Renaming is a single undoable action.
Double-click on the name text starts rename; double-click on the row area outside the name enters isolation mode (for containers).
=== Empty State ===
A document always has at least one layer. Attempting to delete the last remaining layer is prevented (delete action is disabled). New documents start with a single layer named "Layer 1" with the first color in the preset cycle (Light Blue). No special empty-state message or placeholder is shown.
=== Layer Color Presets ===
The nine preset colors, in cycle order for auto-assignment: 1. Light Blue   (#4a90d9) 2. Red          (#d94a4a) 3. Green        (#4ad94a) 4. Blue         (#4a4ad9) 5. Yellow       (#d9d94a) 6. Magenta      (#d94ad9) 7. Cyan         (#4ad9d9) 8. Light Gray   (#b0b0b0) 9. Dark Green   (#2a7a2a)
Auto-assignment cycles through this list in order: Layer 1 gets Light Blue, Layer 2 gets Red, etc., wrapping after 9. The cycle index is per-document and advances with each layer created.
When a layer's color matches a preset, the Layer Options dialog dropdown shows that preset name. Otherwise it displays "Custom." "Custom" is a display-only label, not selectable from the dropdown; the user must click the color swatch to pick a custom color.

magic_wand_panel_content.description:
Magic Wand Panel — configures the five criteria the Magic Wand Tool uses when matching candidate elements against a clicked seed: Fill Color, Stroke Color, Stroke Weight, Opacity, and Blending Mode. Each criterion is independently enabled by a checkbox; tolerances apply only when the corresponding checkbox is on.
Layout (top to bottom, matching the visual grouping in the reference design):
Row 1 — Fill Color checkbox + Tolerance: numeric (0–255). Row 2 — Stroke Color checkbox + Tolerance: numeric (0–255). Row 3 — Stroke Weight checkbox + Tolerance: numeric (pt). Row 4 — spacer (visual separator between the appearance group
  above and the compositing group below).
Row 5 — Opacity checkbox + Tolerance: numeric (%). Row 6 — Blending Mode checkbox (no tolerance — blend mode is a
  categorical enum, exact match only).

Defaults: - Fill / Stroke / Stroke Weight / Opacity: enabled. - Blending Mode: disabled. - Tolerances: 32 / 32 / 5.0 pt / 5%.
See transcripts/MAGIC_WAND_TOOL.md for the predicate and gesture semantics. The actual matching happens inside doc.magic_wand.apply (declared in workspace/actions.yaml or registered as a doc.* effect by each native app).

opacity_panel_content.description:
Opacity panel — sets opacity and blend mode on the current selection, and manages opacity masks on selected elements. See transcripts/OPACITY.md for the full specification.
Flask's phase-1 scope is yaml wiring only — the document model for per-element opacity / blend mode / opacity masks lands in later phases in the native apps. Phase-1 behavior in Flask:

  - MODE_DROPDOWN and OPACITY_INPUT are functional; they bind to panel-
    local `mode` and `opacity` working values.
  - OPACITY_PREVIEW, LINK_INDICATOR, MASK_PREVIEW render as placeholder
    boxes.
  - MAKE_MASK_BUTTON, CLIP_CHECKBOX, INVERT_MASK_CHECKBOX are present but
    disabled until the document model carries element.mask.
  - Mask-lifecycle and page-level menu items use enabled_when: "false"
    until their respective phases land (pending_model for disable_mask /
    unlink_mask; pending_renderer for page_isolated_blending /
    page_knockout_group).

=== Layout ===
Row 1 — MODE_DROPDOWN (4 cols), "Opacity:" label (3 cols), OPACITY_INPUT (4 cols), OPACITY_DISCLOSURE chevron (1 col).
Row 2 — OPACITY_PREVIEW (3 cols), LINK_INDICATOR (1 col), MASK_PREVIEW (3 cols), and a buttons column (5 cols) containing MAKE_MASK_BUTTON, CLIP_CHECKBOX, INVERT_MASK_CHECKBOX. The entire row is hidden when panel.thumbnails_hidden is true.
=== Mode values ===
The 16 blend modes are grouped into six sections by dividers in the dropdown, matching the groups in transcripts/OPACITY.md:

  - [normal]
  - [darken, multiply, color_burn]
  - [lighten, screen, color_dodge]
  - [overlay, soft_light, hard_light]
  - [difference, exclusion]
  - [hue, saturation, color, luminosity]

=== Panel menu ===
Ten entries across four groups. Phase-1 actionable: Hide Thumbnails, Show Options, New Opacity Masks Are Clipping, New Opacity Masks Are Inverted (panel-local toggles). Phase-1 deferred: all mask-lifecycle items and the page-level blending toggles.

blend_mode.description: Working blend mode. Phase-1 is panel-local (not yet applied to elements); later phases persist as element.mode on the selection. Named `blend_mode` rather than `mode` to avoid a collision with the Color panel's state.mode in the shared panel-overrides map.

opacity.description: Working opacity value (0-100). Phase-1 is panel-local (not yet applied to elements); later phases persist as element.opacity on the selection.

thumbnails_hidden.description: When true, the preview row (OPACITY_PREVIEW, LINK_INDICATOR, MASK_PREVIEW, and the mask-buttons column) is collapsed.

options_shown.description: When true, the isolated_blending and knockout_group toggles are revealed inline in the panel body below the preview row. Phase-1 has no inline controls for these yet; the flag is tracked for parity with the panel menu checkmark.

new_masks_clipping.description: Document preference for the initial state of mask.clip on newly made opacity masks. Does not affect existing masks.

new_masks_inverted.description: Document preference for the initial state of mask.invert on newly made opacity masks. Does not affect existing masks.

menu.label: Page Isolated Blending

menu.label: Page Knockout Group

children.description: Blend mode for the selection. Sixteen modes grouped into six sections by separator.
Numeric opacity for the selection, 0-100%.
Opens a popover containing a horizontal opacity slider bound to panel.opacity.

Thumbnail of the selection. Phase-1 renders as a placeholder box in Flask; native apps render actual selection artwork.
Clicking this preview sets the editing target to the element's main content (exits mask-editing mode) per OPACITY.md §Preview interactions. The native renderers draw a persistent highlight on this preview while it is the active editing target (i.e., while ``editing_target_is_mask`` is false).

Icon showing whether the mask's transform is linked to the element's. The glyph flips between a connected chain (``link_linked``) and a broken chain (``link_unlinked``) based on the first selected element's mask.linked. Clicking toggles mask.linked on every selected mask via ``Controller::toggle_mask_linked_on_selection``. Disabled when the selection has no mask. OPACITY.md §Document model.

Thumbnail of the opacity mask (or an empty-mask glyph when no mask exists). Phase-1 renders as a placeholder box.
Clicking this preview enters mask-editing mode per OPACITY.md §Preview interactions: the editing target switches to the first selected element's mask subtree, and subsequent tool drawing adds to ``element.mask.subtree`` instead of the active layer. Requires ``selection_has_mask``; a no-op otherwise. The native renderers draw a persistent highlight on this preview while mask-editing is active (``editing_target_is_mask`` is true).
Creates an opacity mask on the selected element(s), or releases an existing mask. The label flips between "Make Mask" and "Release" based on selection_has_mask per OPACITY.md § States.

paragraph_panel_content.description:
Paragraph formatting panel per PARAGRAPH.md (Phase 2 — static UI). Provides the alignment radio group (7 icon toggles), bullets and numbered-list dropdowns, indent and space-before/after combos, and the hyphenate checkbox. Panel menu opens the Justification and Hyphenation dialogs; Hanging Punctuation is a menu-only checkmark toggle. Bound to panel-local state only; selection-driven wiring lands in Phase 3, paragraph attribute writes in Phase 4.
Layout follows PARAGRAPH.md §Layout — bootstrap-style grid with intentional right-padding on three rows (alignment: 5-col padding; first-line indent: 6-col padding; hyphenate: 9-col padding).

align_left.description: ALIGN_LEFT_BUTTON state. Default for LTR text.

justify_left.description: JUSTIFY_LEFT_BUTTON — justify body, last line left.

justify_center.description: JUSTIFY_CENTER_BUTTON — justify body, last line centred.

area_text_selected.description: True when every selected text element is area text (a wrapping frame, width > 0 and height > 0). Drives bind.disabled on area-only controls (JUSTIFY_*, indents, hyphenate, hanging punctuation). Native apps overwrite on selection change.

children.label: Hyphenate
