properties_panel_content.description: Transform and appearance properties for the selected object(s): position (X, Y), size (W, H) with constrain-proportions lock, rotation angle, shear, opacity slider, and blend mode dropdown. When multiple objects are selected, shows shared values or blank for mixed values. All fields are editable and apply immediately.

stroke_panel_content.description:
Stroke attribute controls for the selected path(s). The panel provides controls for stroke weight, endpoint cap style, corner join style with miter limit, stroke alignment relative to the path, dashed line patterns, arrowheads with independent start/end shape and scale, arrow alignment mode, and variable-width stroke profiles.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 — Weight. A label and a number input for the stroke weight in points. Accepts any non-negative value including decimals.
Row 2 — Cap. Three mutually exclusive icon toggle buttons for line endpoint style: butt (flat at the endpoint), round (semicircular beyond the endpoint), and square (extends beyond the endpoint by half the stroke width). Default is butt.
Row 3 — Corner. Three mutually exclusive icon toggle buttons for line join style: miter (sharp corner), round (circular arc), and bevel (flat diagonal). Followed by a miter limit label and numeric input. The miter limit controls when a miter join auto-converts to bevel on acute angles; the value is the ratio of miter length to stroke width. Default miter limit is 10. The miter limit field is disabled when the join style is round or bevel.
Row 4 — Align Stroke. Three mutually exclusive icon toggle buttons controlling where the stroke is drawn relative to the path: center (stroke straddles the path, the default), inside (stroke drawn inside closed paths), or outside (stroke drawn outside closed paths). Inside and outside behave the same as center on open paths.
Row 5 — Dashed Line. A checkbox to enable dashing, plus two mutually-exclusive icon buttons that select how the dash pattern is applied to the path: preserve (the default — dashes lay out by exact length along the path; corners and ends fall wherever the math lands) and align-to-anchors (per-segment dash/gap lengths flex slightly so a dash is centered on every anchor, and a full dash sits at each open path end). The full algorithm and persistence model are documented in DASH_ALIGN.md.
Row 6 — Dash/gap pattern. Six numeric inputs arranged as three dash/gap pairs. Pair 1 defaults to 12pt/12pt. Pairs 2 and 3 default to blank (unused — not part of the pattern). A value of 0 in a dash position produces a zero-length segment (a dot with round caps). The effective dash array is built left to right from non-blank pairs. All six fields are disabled (grayed out) when the dashed line checkbox is unchecked. The values persist and reappear when re-checked.
Row 7 — Arrowheads. A label, two dropdowns (start and end) selecting from 15 arrowhead shapes, and a swap button that exchanges start and end arrowhead selections (both shape and scale). Both dropdowns share the same shape list. The renderer flips the shape based on which end it is placed on.
Arrowhead shapes: none (default), simple_arrow (filled triangle), open_arrow (unfilled triangle), closed_arrow (filled triangle with bar at base), stealth_arrow (sharp swept-back chevron, filled), barbed_arrow (curved swept-back, filled), half_arrow_upper (upper half of filled triangle), half_arrow_lower (lower half of filled triangle), circle (filled), open_circle (outline), square (filled), open_square (outline), diamond (filled diamond/rhombus), open_diamond (outline diamond), slash (perpendicular line across the path).
Row 8 — Scale. A label, two editable combo boxes (start and end) for arrowhead scale as a percentage, and a link/unlink toggle button (chain icon). Preset dropdown values: 50%, 75%, 100%, 150%, 200%, 300%, 400%. The user can also type any positive value (minimum 1%). Default is 100%. Scale is relative to the stroke weight. When the link button is active, changing either scale updates both. Default is unlinked.
Row 9 — Arrow Align. A label and two mutually exclusive icon toggle buttons: tip-at-end (the arrowhead tip sits exactly at the path endpoint; the body extends inward along the path) and center-at-end (the arrowhead center sits at the path endpoint; the tip extends beyond). Default is tip-at-end.
Row 10 — Profile. A dropdown selecting a variable-width stroke profile, plus flip and reset buttons. Profiles: uniform (constant width, the default), taper_both (tapers at both ends), taper_start (tapers at start), taper_end (tapers at end), bulge (wider in the middle), pinch (narrower in the middle). Flip mirrors the profile along the path. Reset restores uniform.

weight.description: Stroke weight in points. Non-negative, decimals allowed.

cap.description: Line cap style. Butt: flat at the endpoint. Round: semicircular beyond the endpoint. Square: extends beyond the endpoint by half the stroke width.

join.description: Line join style. Miter: sharp pointed corner. Round: circular arc. Bevel: flat diagonal cut.

miter_limit.description: Miter length / stroke width ratio. When exceeded, the miter join auto-converts to bevel. Minimum 1. Only effective when join is miter.

align_stroke.description: Stroke alignment relative to the path. Center: stroke straddles the path. Inside: stroke drawn inside closed paths. Outside: stroke drawn outside closed paths. Inside and outside behave as center on open paths.

dash_1.description: Dash length for pair 1, in points.

gap_1.description: Gap length for pair 1, in points.

dash_2.description: Dash length for pair 2, in points. Null means unused.

gap_2.description: Gap length for pair 2, in points. Null means unused.

dash_3.description: Dash length for pair 3, in points. Null means unused.

gap_3.description: Gap length for pair 3, in points. Null means unused.

dash_align_anchors.description: When true, per-segment dash and gap lengths flex so a dash is centered on every anchor and a full dash sits at each open path end. When false (default), the dash pattern lays out by exact length along the path. Mutually exclusive with the preserve mode represented by the alternate Row 5 button. See DASH_ALIGN.md.

start_arrowhead.description: Arrowhead shape at the start of the path.

end_arrowhead.description: Arrowhead shape at the end of the path.

start_arrowhead_scale.description: Arrowhead scale at the start of the path, as a percentage of the stroke weight. Minimum 1. Presets: 50, 75, 100, 150, 200, 300, 400. Free entry allowed.

end_arrowhead_scale.description: Arrowhead scale at the end of the path, as a percentage of the stroke weight. Minimum 1. Presets: 50, 75, 100, 150, 200, 300, 400. Free entry allowed.

link_arrowhead_scale.description: When true, changing either arrowhead scale updates both to the same value.

arrow_align.description: Arrow alignment mode. Tip-at-end: the arrowhead tip sits exactly at the path endpoint, body extends inward. Center-at-end: the arrowhead center sits at the endpoint, tip extends beyond.

profile.description: Variable-width stroke profile. Uniform: constant width. Taper_both: tapers at both ends. Taper_start/taper_end: tapers at one end. Bulge: wider in the middle. Pinch: narrower in the middle.

profile_flipped.description: Whether the stroke profile is flipped (mirrored along the path). Only meaningful for asymmetric profiles (taper_start, taper_end).

children.description: Stroke weight as a length. Bare numbers and decimals are interpreted in pt; a unit suffix overrides (`12 px`, `0.5 mm`, `1 in`, `2 pc`). Committed on Enter or focus loss.
Flat endpoint — stroke ends exactly at the path endpoint.
Semicircular endpoint — stroke extends beyond the path endpoint with a half-circle.
Projecting endpoint — stroke extends beyond the path endpoint by half the stroke weight.
Sharp pointed corner. May auto-convert to bevel when the miter limit is exceeded.
Circular arc at the corner.
Flat diagonal cut at the corner.
Miter length / stroke width ratio. When the ratio of a miter join exceeds this value, the join auto-converts to bevel. Minimum 1. Disabled when join style is round or bevel.
Stroke straddles the path, half on each side.
Stroke drawn inside the path boundary. Behaves as center on open paths.
Stroke drawn outside the path boundary. Behaves as center on open paths.
Enable dashed stroke. When unchecked, the dash/gap fields are disabled but retain their values.
children.label: Dashed Line

children.description: Dashes lay out by exact length along the path; corners and path ends fall wherever the math lands. Matches plain SVG semantics.

children.summary: Align dashes to corners and path ends
children.description: Per-segment dash/gap lengths flex slightly so a dash is centered on every anchor and a full dash sits at each open path end. Anchor means any path node, smooth or corner. See DASH_ALIGN.md for the algorithm.
Dash length for pair 1. Bare values and decimals are interpreted in pt; a unit suffix overrides (`12 px`, `0.5 mm`, `1 in`, `2 pc`). 0 produces a dot with round caps.
Gap length for pair 1. Same unit grammar as the dash inputs.
Dash length for pair 2. Same unit grammar as pair 1; blank means unused.
Gap length for pair 2. Same unit grammar as pair 1; blank means unused.
Dash length for pair 3. Same unit grammar as pair 1; blank means unused.
Gap length for pair 3. Same unit grammar as pair 1; blank means unused.
Arrowhead shape at the start of the path. The renderer flips the shape so it points outward from the path start.
Arrowhead shape at the end of the path. The renderer flips the shape so it points outward from the path end.
Swap start and end arrowhead selections, including both the shape and the scale values.
Scale of the start arrowhead as a percentage of the stroke weight. Dropdown presets plus free entry. Minimum 1%. Committed on Enter or focus loss. Invalid input reverts to the previous value.
Scale of the end arrowhead as a percentage of the stroke weight. Dropdown presets plus free entry. Minimum 1%. Committed on Enter or focus loss. Invalid input reverts to the previous value.
Toggle linked arrowhead scales. When linked (active), changing either scale updates both to the same value.
The arrowhead tip sits exactly at the path endpoint. The arrowhead body extends inward along the path.
The arrowhead center sits at the path endpoint. The tip extends beyond the endpoint.
Variable-width stroke profile. Controls how the stroke weight varies along the length of the path.
Mirror the stroke profile along the path. Toggles the profile_flipped state. Only visually meaningful for asymmetric profiles (taper_start, taper_end).
Reset the stroke profile to uniform and clear the flipped state.

swatches_panel_content.description:
Swatch library panel for managing named color swatches organized into libraries. The panel displays a fill/stroke widget, a recent-colors row, and one or more swatch libraries rendered as collapsible sections with wrapping color tiles.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 -- Fill/stroke widget. Same visual behavior as the toolbar widget except single-click only (no double-click to open the modal picker from within the panel).
Row 2 -- "Recent Colors" label.
Row 3 -- Recent colors. Same per-document recent colors as the Color panel: ten 16px slots, newest first. Click sets the active color. Double-click opens the Swatch Options dialog in create mode.
Row 4 -- Swatch tiles area. Each loaded library is rendered as a collapsible section: a disclosure triangle + library name label, followed by swatches wrapping left-to-right, top-to-bottom. The tile size is controlled by the thumbnail_size panel state (small=16px, medium=32px, large=64px).
Single-click a library swatch to select it and set the active color. Double-click opens the Swatch Options dialog in edit mode. Shift+click adds a range to the selection. Cmd+click toggles individual swatch selection. Multiple swatches can be selected.
Each swatch has: name (string), color (hex), color_mode (grayscale/rgb/ hsb/cmyk/web_safe_rgb), color_type ("process"), global (bool, deferred).
Libraries are loaded from workspace/swatches/*.json at startup. The default library is "Web Colors" (216 web-safe colors).

thumbnail_size.description: Swatch tile display size. Small=16px, Medium=32px, Large=64px.

selected_library.description: ID of the library containing the most recent swatch selection. Used as the target for New Swatch, Sort, Save, etc.

selected_swatches.description: List of selected swatch indices within the selected library.

open_libraries.description: List of open libraries with their collapsed state. Each entry has an "id" (library filename stem) and "collapsed" (bool).

recent_colors.description: Per-document list of recently committed colors, newest first. Shared with the Color panel.

menu.label: New Swatch
menu.description: Appends a new swatch to the selected library, initialized from the current active color (fill or stroke, per fill_on_top). Opens the Swatch Options dialog in create mode so the user can edit the name, color mode, and color before confirming. The new swatch is appended at the end of the library's swatch list.

menu.label: Duplicate Swatch
menu.description: Create a copy of each currently selected swatch in the selected library. The copies are inserted immediately after their originals with " copy" appended to the name. The copies become the new selection. Disabled when no swatches are selected.

menu.label: Delete Swatch
menu.description: Remove the currently selected swatch(es) from the selected library. The deletion is permanent (no undo). The selection is cleared after deletion. Disabled when no swatches are selected.

menu.label: Select All Unused
menu.description: Select every swatch in the panel whose color does not appear anywhere in the current document as a fill or stroke color on any object. Useful for identifying swatches that can be safely deleted. The selection replaces any existing selection.

menu.label: Add Used Colors
menu.description: Scan every object in the current document and collect the set of unique fill and stroke colors in use. For each color that is not already present in the selected library, create a new swatch and append it to the end. Swatch names default to the "R=N G=N B=N" component format. Colors are compared by hex value.

menu.label: Sort by Name
menu.description: Permanently reorder the swatches in the selected library alphabetically by swatch name (case-sensitive, lexicographic). This modifies the library data in place. The reordered sequence is preserved when the library is saved.

menu.label: Small Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display swatch tiles at 16x16 pixels. This is the smallest view, matching the size of recent-color swatches. Radio button group with Medium and Large; a checkmark indicates the active size.

menu.label: Medium Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display swatch tiles at 32x32 pixels. Radio button group with Small and Large; a checkmark indicates the active size.

menu.label: Large Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display swatch tiles at 64x64 pixels. This is the largest view, showing colors with enough area to distinguish subtle variations. Radio button group with Small and Medium; a checkmark indicates the active size.

menu.label: Swatch Options...
menu.description: Open the Swatch Options dialog in edit mode for the first selected swatch. The dialog displays the swatch's name, color type (always "Process Color"), color mode, color component sliders, and hex value. OK updates the swatch in place. Disabled when no swatches are selected.

menu.label: Open Swatch Library
menu.description: Submenu listing all available swatch libraries discovered from workspace/swatches/*.json files. Selecting a library adds it to the panel's open_libraries list, displaying it as a new collapsible section in the swatch tiles area. Libraries that are already open are shown with a checkmark. Each library appears as a disclosure section with a triangle toggle and the library name.

menu.label: Save Swatch Library
menu.description: Open the Save Swatch Library dialog, which prompts for a library name. The selected library's swatches are saved to workspace/swatches/ as a JSON file. The file contains the library name, description, and the full swatch array (name, color, color mode, color type, global flag for each swatch).

children.description:
Scrollable area displaying all open swatch libraries. Each library is rendered as a collapsible disclosure section: a disclosure triangle (click to expand/collapse) followed by the library name label. When expanded, the library's swatches are displayed in a wrapping left-to-right, top-to-bottom layout (like text in a paragraph).
Multiple libraries can be open simultaneously, stacked vertically. The list of open libraries and their collapsed state is stored in panel.open_libraries. New libraries are added via the Open Swatch Library submenu in the panel menu.
Swatch tile size is controlled by panel.thumbnail_size (small=16px, medium=32px, large=64px), defaulting to small (16px) to match the recent-color swatches.
Interaction model: Single-click a swatch to select it and set the active fill/stroke color (per fill_on_top). Selection uses the generic select effect with mode=auto: plain click for single selection, Shift+click to extend a range, Cmd/Ctrl+click to toggle. The selected_swatches panel state tracks the selection as a list of indices within the selected_library. Visual selection feedback is provided by the data-bind-selected-in binding, which adds a jas-selected CSS class (2px accent-colored outline).
Double-click a swatch to open the Swatch Options dialog in edit mode, passing the swatch's name, color, color_mode, library id, and index as dialog parameters.
This area uses the generic repeat directive to iterate over panel.open_libraries (outer repeat, producing disclosure sections) and data.swatch_libraries[lib.id].swatches (inner repeat, producing color_swatch tiles). The bracket notation [lib.id] resolves the outer repeat variable within the inner repeat's data source path.

appearance_save_as.description: Text input dialog for naming a custom appearance. The current resolved appearance (all colors, fonts, and sizes) is saved to browser storage under the given name. Save is disabled when the name field is empty.

apply_to_strokes_confirm.description:
Confirmation sub-dialog opened by brush_options_confirm in library_edit mode. Two-way per BRUSH_OPTIONS_DIALOG.md §Apply to Strokes confirm dialog (no Leave Strokes branch).
Apply: library mutation commits and every element with
  jas:stroke-brush referencing this brush is re-rendered. The
  parent Brush Options dialog closes.
Cancel: library mutation is discarded; the parent Brush Options
  dialog stays open with the user's edits still pending.

Spec carries the library + brush_slug + patch (the dialog state delta) so the Apply effect chain can dispatch through brush.update and re-render selection.

children.content: The brush "{{param.brush_name}}" has been modified. Apply changes to existing strokes using this brush?

artboard_options.description:
Modal dialog for editing the properties of a single artboard. ARTBOARDS.md §Artboard Options Dialogue.
Batched commit: edits don't apply until OK; Cancel discards; a single undo entry records the OK (via snapshot inside the confirm action, plus the dialog-snapshot preview-restore for reversible preview_targets).
Layout follows the reference image: Name / Preset rows, then a pair of rows with Width / Height on the left and X / Y on the right (with the reference-point widget and chain-link toggle in the middle column). Orientation button and Fill selector share the next row. Display section groups the three show_* toggles plus the Video Ruler Pixel Aspect Ratio input. Global section carries the document-wide fade_region_outside_artboard toggle with the indented update_while_dragging toggle. Info section displays the live Artboards count. Footer: Delete / Cancel / OK.
Phase-1 deferrals: CHAIN_LINK_BUTTON state persists in dialog state but does not alter Width / Height edits (drag-resize is not wired). update_while_dragging persists but has no observable effect in Flask. video_ruler_pixel_aspect_ratio round-trips but does not distort the canvas.

x_rp.set: fun new -> x_stored := new - anchor_offset_x(panel.reference_point, width)

y_rp.set: fun new -> y_stored := new - anchor_offset_y(panel.reference_point, height)

behavior.description: Selecting a preset sets dialog.width and dialog.height to the preset's dimensions. "Custom" is display-only (cannot be selected) and appears automatically when W/H match no preset. Native apps translate the selected value into W/H assignments.

children.description: Toggle — when engaged, W and H scale proportionally. Dialog-session-only; default off on each open.
3×3 grid; exactly one anchor active. Changes X / Y display, not storage.

bind.value: if dialog.fill == 'transparent' then 'transparent' else if dialog.fill == '#ffffff' then 'white' else if dialog.fill == '#000000' then 'black' else 'custom'

children.description: Click to open the Color Picker for a custom fill.

blob_brush_tool_options.description:
Modal dialog opened by double-clicking the Blob Brush icon in the toolbar. Edits six per-document options that drive the Blob Brush tool's sweep and commit behaviour:
- Fidelity: 5-stop slider (Accurate ↔ Smooth) driving the
  RDP simplification epsilon at commit. Tick 1 = 0.5,
  tick 3 (default) = 5.0, tick 5 = 10.0. See
  BLOB_BRUSH_TOOL.md §Fidelity → simplification tolerance
  mapping.
- Keep Selected: when on, the new / merged element becomes
  the canvas selection after commit. Default false.
- Merge Only with Selection: when on, painting-mode sweeps
  merge only with existing Blob Brush elements in the
  selection; erasing-mode is unaffected.
- Size / Angle / Roundness: default tip shape values used
  when no Calligraphic brush is active. Each is a variation
  widget (fixed / random / pressure / tilt / bearing /
  rotation). Rotation mode is disabled for size and
  roundness. When a Calligraphic brush IS active in the
  Brushes panel, these rows are disabled with a "(set by
  active brush)" hint — the brush's own values take
  precedence. See §Runtime tip resolution.

Reset restores defaults without committing. Cancel discards edits. OK writes all six values to state.blob_brush_*. See BLOB_BRUSH_TOOL.md.

fidelity.description: 5-stop slider position (1-5); maps to RDP epsilon

keep_selected.description: New / merged element becomes canvas selection after commit

merge_only_with_selection.description: Painting-mode merge scoped to the current selection

boolean_options.description:
Modal dialog reached from the Boolean panel's hamburger menu via "Boolean Options…". Edits three document-level preferences that every boolean operation consults:
- Precision: single tolerance (in points) for Bezier flattening,
  Bezier refit, and redundant-point collapse. Default 0.0283 pt
  (= 0.01 mm).
- Remove Redundant Points: when on, collinear points in boolean-
  op output whose deviation is within Precision are collapsed.
- Divide and Outline Will Remove Unpainted Artwork: when on,
  DIVIDE fragments with no fill and no stroke are discarded
  rather than kept as invisible paths.

Cancel dismisses the dialog without applying; OK writes the three fields to document state and dismisses. See BOOLEAN.md §Boolean Options dialog.

remove_redundant_points.description: Collapse near-collinear points in boolean-op output

divide_remove_unpainted.description: DIVIDE drops fragments with no paint when on

children.label: Divide and Outline Will Remove Unpainted Artwork

brush_options.description:
Modal dialog for creating, editing, or per-instance overriding a brush. Three modes selected by param.mode:
create — opened by NEW_BRUSH_BUTTON or the New Brush menu item. BRUSH_TYPE_RADIO is enabled; the user picks a type and configures the per-type body. OK appends a new brush to the selected library in panel.selected_library.
library_edit — opened by double-click on a brush tile or the Brush Options... menu item. BRUSH_TYPE_RADIO is disabled; the body shows the selected brush's parameters seeded from data.brush_libraries[param.library].brushes[param.index]. OK mutates the master brush in place and prompts the Apply to Strokes confirm sub-dialog.
instance_edit — opened by BRUSH_OPTIONS_FOR_SELECTION_BUTTON. BRUSH_TYPE_RADIO is disabled; the body is seeded from the selected element's stroke_brush_overrides layered over the master brush. OK writes stroke_brush_overrides on the selected element. No Apply to Strokes prompt.
Phase 1 ships the Calligraphic body only. Scatter, Art, Pattern, and Bristle bodies follow as their data models and renderers land. See transcripts/BRUSH_OPTIONS_DIALOG.md.

brush_type.description: Brush type. Editable only when mode == create; disabled in library_edit and instance_edit (conversion between types is not supported).

or dialog.roundness_variation.mode == "rotation"

dialog.size_variation.mode == "pressure"
or dialog.size_variation.mode == "tilt"
or dialog.size_variation.mode == "bearing"
or dialog.size_variation.mode == "rotation"

color_picker.description: Modal color picker dialog matching the standard vector illustration application layout. Left column: saturation/brightness gradient + hue colorbar + web-safe checkbox. Center column: HSB fields with radio selectors + color preview swatch, RGB fields, CMYK fields, hex field. Right column: OK, Cancel, Color Swatches buttons. The radio buttons select which color channel the 2D gradient and colorbar visualize — e.g. selecting H maps hue to the colorbar and S/B to the gradient axes; selecting R maps red to the colorbar and G/B to the gradient axes.

radio_channel.description: Which color channel the gradient/colorbar visualize

h.set: fun v -> color <- hsb(v, hsb_s(color), hsb_b(color))

s.set: fun v -> color <- hsb(hsb_h(color), v, hsb_b(color))

b.set: fun v -> color <- hsb(hsb_h(color), hsb_s(color), v)

r.set: fun v -> color <- rgb(v, rgb_g(color), rgb_b(color))

g.set: fun v -> color <- rgb(rgb_r(color), v, rgb_b(color))

bl.set: fun v -> let r = rgb_r(color) in let g = rgb_g(color) in color <- rgb(r, g, v)

Square 2D gradient for the color picker. In HSB-H mode (default): horizontal = saturation, vertical = brightness, colored by the current hue. Click or drag to set the two axis values. Circle indicator shows current position.
Vertical bar showing a rainbow hue gradient. Click or drag to select a hue value (0-360). Arrow indicator shows current position.

children.description: Snap all color values to the web-safe palette (0, 51, 102, 153, 204, 255)
50x50px swatch showing the working color in real time

eyedropper_tool_options.description:
Modal dialog opened by double-clicking the Eyedropper icon in the toolbar. Edits 25 per-document toggles that gate which attribute groups and sub-attributes the Eyedropper samples and applies.
Five master groups (Fill, Stroke, Opacity, Character, Paragraph) each have a master toggle; Stroke / Opacity / Character / Paragraph also have sub-toggles for finer control. Master OFF skips the entire group regardless of sub-toggles. Master ON + sub OFF skips that sub-attribute. Both must be ON for an attribute to be copied.
When the source element lacks an attribute (e.g. a Path being sampled has no character data) or the target can't receive it (e.g. applying character attrs to a Path), the corresponding sub-toggles are no-ops at apply time. No warning, no error.
Reset restores all toggles to their declared defaults (all true) without committing. Cancel discards edits. OK writes every working-copy value to the corresponding state.eyedropper_* key.
See transcripts/EYEDROPPER_TOOL.md.

layer_options.description:
Modal dialog for editing or creating a layer. Supports two modes, determined by the param.mode parameter:
Edit mode (param.mode == "edit"): Opened by double-clicking a layer row's options area or choosing "Options for Layer..." from the panel menu or context menu. All fields are populated from the layer's current properties. OK updates the layer in place.
Create mode (param.mode == "create"): Opened by choosing "New Layer..." from the panel menu. Name defaults to "Layer N" (skipping numbers already in use). Color defaults to the next color in the nine-color preset cycle. OK creates a new layer inserted above the topmost panel-selected layer, or at the top of the stack if nothing is panel-selected.
Layout (top to bottom): - Name: label (col-3) + text input (col-9) - Color: label (col-3) + dropdown of 9 preset colors plus "Custom"
  display-only label (col-6) + color swatch that opens Color Picker
  (col-3). When a preset is selected, the swatch updates to match.
  If the current color does not match any preset, the dropdown
  displays "Custom." If a custom color matches a preset, the
  dropdown switches to that preset name.
- Row: empty (col-3) + Template checkbox (col-3, disabled) + Lock
  checkbox (col-3)
- Row: empty (col-3) + Show checkbox (col-3) + Print checkbox
  (col-3, disabled)
- Row: empty (col-3) + Preview checkbox (col-3, disabled when Show
  unchecked) + Dim Images to checkbox with percentage input (col-3)
- Bottom: Cancel button + OK button (right-aligned)
Visibility mapping: The Show and Preview checkboxes map to the three-state visibility model: - Show unchecked -> invisible (Preview is disabled) - Show checked + Preview checked -> preview - Show checked + Preview unchecked -> outline

color_preset.description: Selected color preset, or 'custom' if no preset matches

preview.description: Preview mode (true) or outline mode (false). Disabled when show is false.

dim_images.description: Dim raster images in this layer

dim_percentage.description: Opacity percentage for dimmed raster images

behavior.description: When a preset is selected, update dialog.color to the preset's hex value. The "Custom" option is display-only and cannot be selected by the user; it appears automatically when the swatch color does not match any preset.

children.description: Click to open the Color Picker to set a custom layer color.

paintbrush_tool_options.description:
Modal dialog opened by double-clicking the Paintbrush icon in the toolbar. Edits five per-document options that drive the Paintbrush tool's drawing and edit gestures:
- Fidelity: 5-stop slider (Accurate ↔ Smooth) that maps to the
  fit_curve tolerance (pt) used on release. Tick 1 = 0.5,
  tick 3 (default) = 5.0, tick 5 = 10.0. See
  PAINTBRUSH_TOOL.md §Tool options.
- Fill new brush strokes: when on, new paths get fill =
  state.fill_color. When off, fill = none. Does not affect
  edit-gesture commits.
- Keep Selected: when on, the new path becomes the canvas
  selection after commit.
- Edit Selected Paths: when on, Option/Alt-drag within
  `edit_within` px of a selected path rewrites the nearest
  sub-segment of that path instead of starting a new stroke.
- Within: proximity threshold (px) for the edit gesture;
  dual-bound slider + number input.

Reset restores defaults without committing. Cancel discards edits. OK writes all five values to state.paintbrush_*. See PAINTBRUSH_TOOL.md.

fidelity.description: 5-stop slider position (1-5); maps to fit_error via PAINTBRUSH_TOOL.md §Fidelity → fit_error mapping

fill_new_strokes.description: New strokes get fill = state.fill_color when true

keep_selected.description: New path becomes the canvas selection after commit

edit_selected_paths.description: Option/Alt-drag near a selected path rewrites it

edit_within.description: Proximity threshold (px) for the edit gesture

paragraph_hyphenation.description:
Modal dialog for fine-grained hyphenation controls per PARAGRAPH.md §Hyphenation Dialog. Opened from the Paragraph panel menu (Hyphenation…).
Layout (top to bottom): - Master checkbox: "Hyphenation" (gates the rest of the dialog —
  when off, all sibling controls render dimmed/non-interactive
  via bind.disabled = "not dialog.hyphenate")
- 5 numeric input rows: Words Longer Than / After First /
  Before Last / Hyphen Limit / Hyphenation Zone
- 7-step discrete slider with endpoint labels (Better Spacing /
  Fewer Hyphens)
- Hyphenate Capitalized Words checkbox - Footer: Preview | Cancel | OK
The master HYPHENATE_MASTER_CHECKBOX mirrors the Paragraph panel's HYPHENATE_CHECKBOX (both write panel.hyphenate). The OK action commits the master and the 7 sub-fields onto every paragraph wrapper tspan in the selection (per-app apply_hyphenation_dialog_to_selection handles the identity- value rule, omitting attrs that match the spec defaults). The apply path also writes the master back to panel.hyphenate so the main panel checkbox reflects the dialog commit. Cancel closes without committing.
Preview-on rollback is still on the to-do list: the Phase 0 snapshot harness only captures top-level state keys, and this dialog needs a per-wrapper attr snapshot so Cancel can restore live-applied edits. Until that lands, leave Preview off when you might want to bail out.
Defaults per PARAGRAPH.md §Reset Panel: master off, 3 / 1 / 1 letters, 0 hyphens (unlimited), 0 pt zone, bias 0 (Better Spacing), capitalized off.
The hyphenation language is inherited from the character-level xml:lang (set via LANGUAGE_DROPDOWN in CHARACTER.md); this dialog has no language control of its own.

hyphenate.description: HYPHENATE_MASTER_CHECKBOX — master switch. When off, every other field in the dialog renders dimmed/non-interactive (via bind.disabled). The apply pipeline mirrors this back to panel.hyphenate so the main Paragraph panel checkbox stays in sync with the dialog commit.

hyphenate_min_word.description: Words Longer Than N letters. jas:hyphenate-min-word.

hyphenate_zone.description: Hyphenation Zone in pt — distance from the right margin within which hyphenation is considered (non-justified paragraphs only). jas:hyphenate-zone.

hyphenate_bias.description: Bias slider — discrete 7-step (0 = Better Spacing / cheap hyphens; 6 = Fewer Hyphens / expensive hyphens). Wires into the every-line composer hyphen penalty as part of the layout work that consumes the Knuth-Liang break points landed in Phase 9. jas:hyphenate-bias.

preview.description: Preview checkbox. When on, every edit live-applies to the selected paragraphs. Cancel always rolls back to the on-open snapshot via the Phase 0 preview snapshot/restore harness.

paragraph_justification.description:
Modal dialog for fine-grained paragraph justification controls per PARAGRAPH.md §Justification Dialog. Opened from the Paragraph panel menu (Justification…).
Layout (top to bottom): - 3×3 grid: Word Spacing / Letter Spacing / Glyph Scaling rows
  × Minimum / Desired / Maximum columns
- separator (spec's `.hr`) - Auto Leading: row (label col-6 + numeric input col-3) - Single Word Justification: row (label col-6 + dropdown col-4) - Footer: Preview checkbox (left) + Cancel button + OK button
  (right-aligned)

Dialog-local state holds the 11 justification fields. OK commits them onto every paragraph wrapper tspan in the selection (per-app apply functions handle the identity-value rule, omitting attrs that match the spec defaults). Cancel closes without committing.
Preview-on rollback is still on the to-do list: the Phase 0 snapshot harness only captures top-level state keys, and the Justification dialog needs a per-wrapper attr snapshot so Cancel can restore live-applied edits. Until that lands, leave Preview off when you might want to bail out.
Defaults per PARAGRAPH.md §Reset Panel: word spacing 80 / 100 / 133 %; letter spacing 0 / 0 / 0 %; glyph scaling 100 / 100 / 100 %; auto leading 120 %; single word justification = Full Justify.

word_spacing_min.description: WORD_SPACING_MIN_VALUE — percent. Stored as jas:word-spacing-min on the paragraph wrapper tspan.

auto_leading.description: AUTO_LEADING_VALUE — percent of font size for Auto leading. Overrides the global 120% default from CHARACTER.md on the paragraphs in the current selection. Stored as jas:auto-leading.

single_word_justify.description: SINGLE_WORD_JUSTIFY_DROPDOWN — how to render a justified line containing only one word. Default Full Justify (stretches the single word across the full width). Stored as jas:single-word-justify.

preview.description: Preview checkbox. When on, every edit live-applies to the selected paragraphs. Cancel always rolls back to the on-open snapshot via the Phase 0 preview snapshot/restore harness. Defaults to off each time the dialog opens.

rotate_options.description:
Modal dialog opened by double-clicking the Rotate icon in the toolbar OR by Alt-clicking on the canvas (which also sets the reference point at the click coordinate). Edits the per-document options that drive the Rotate tool's transformation:
- Angle: rotation in degrees, applied around the current
  reference point. Positive values rotate counter-clockwise.
- Preview: when on, the canvas re-renders live with the
  dialog's current angle applied (no document mutation
  until OK).

Reset restores defaults without committing. Cancel discards edits and any preview overlay (the reference point set by an Alt-click is NOT reverted). OK writes values to state.rotate_* and runs doc.rotate.apply on the selection. Copy runs doc.rotate.apply with copy=true, applying the rotation to a duplicate of the selection (placed immediately above the original in z-order; selection moves to the duplicate). See ROTATE_TOOL.md.

preview.description: Live-update the canvas with dialog values applied

save_changes_tab.description: Confirmation dialog shown when closing a tab with unsaved changes. Three options: Save the document, Discard changes, or Cancel.

save_changes_window.description: Confirmation dialog shown when closing the window with one or more modified documents. Four options: Save All, Save (active only), Don't Save, Cancel.

children.content: You have unsaved changes. Save before closing?

scale_options.description:
Modal dialog opened by double-clicking the Scale icon in the toolbar OR by Alt-clicking on the canvas (which also sets the reference point at the click coordinate). Edits the per-document options that drive the Scale tool's transformation:
- Scale mode: Uniform applies a single percentage to both axes;
  Non-Uniform applies independent Horizontal and Vertical
  percentages.
- Scale Strokes: when on, stroke widths multiply by the
  geometric mean √(|sx · sy|). When off, stroke widths are
  preserved.
- Scale Corners: when on, rounded_rect rx/ry scale by
  |sx|/|sy| respectively. No-op on other element types.
- Preview: when on, the canvas re-renders live with the
  dialog's current values applied (no document mutation
  until OK).

Reset restores defaults without committing. Cancel discards edits and any preview overlay (the reference point set by an Alt-click is NOT reverted). OK writes all values to state.scale_* and runs doc.scale.apply on the selection. Copy runs doc.scale.apply with copy=true, applying the transformation to a duplicate of the selection (placed immediately above the original in z-order; selection moves to the duplicate). See SCALE_TOOL.md.

uniform.description: Uniform mode (single percentage) vs Non-Uniform (independent H/V)

scale_strokes.description: Multiply stroke widths by the geometric mean of (sx, sy)

scale_corners.description: Scale rounded_rect rx/ry along with the geometry

preview.description: Live-update the canvas with dialog values applied

shear_options.description:
Modal dialog opened by double-clicking the Shear icon in the toolbar OR by Alt-clicking on the canvas (which also sets the reference point at the click coordinate). Edits the per-document options that drive the Shear tool's transformation:
- Shear Angle: slant angle in degrees. Positive values shear
  in the +axis_perp direction.
- Axis: direction along which points slide. Horizontal (x-axis
  fixed), Vertical (y-axis fixed), or Custom (user-specified
  angle in degrees from horizontal). Custom Angle is enabled
  only when Custom is selected.
- Preview: when on, the canvas re-renders live with the
  dialog's current angle/axis applied (no document mutation
  until OK).

Reset restores defaults without committing. Cancel discards edits and any preview overlay (the reference point set by an Alt-click is NOT reverted). OK writes values to state.shear_* and runs doc.shear.apply on the selection. Copy runs doc.shear.apply with copy=true, applying the shear to a duplicate of the selection (placed immediately above the original in z-order; selection moves to the duplicate). See SHEAR_TOOL.md.

axis_angle.description: Custom-axis angle in degrees from horizontal (used only when axis = custom)

preview.description: Live-update the canvas with dialog values applied

swatch_library_save.description: Modal dialog for saving the selected swatch library to a file. The dialog presents a single text input for the library name. On confirm (Save button, dispatches save_swatch_library_confirm), the selected library's complete data (name, description, and all swatches with their name, color, color_mode, color_type, and global fields) is serialized to JSON and written to workspace/swatches/<name>.json. Save is disabled when the name field is empty.

swatch_options.description:
Modal dialog for editing or creating a named color swatch. Supports two modes, determined by the param.mode parameter:
Edit mode (param.mode == "edit"): Opened by double-clicking a library swatch tile or choosing Swatch Options... from the panel menu. All fields are populated from the selected swatch's data (name, color, color_mode) passed as dialog parameters. The color component sliders and hex field are initialized from param.color using the color conversion functions. OK updates the swatch in its library at the specified index.
Create mode (param.mode == "create"): Opened by double-clicking a non-library swatch (recent color, fill/stroke widget), choosing New Swatch from the panel menu, or choosing Create New Swatch... from the Color Panel menu. Fields are initialized from the active fill/stroke color. OK appends a new swatch to the selected library.
Layout (top to bottom): - Swatch Name: text input (col-3 label / col-9 input) - Color Type: dropdown, always "Process Color" (disabled, future use) - Global: checkbox (centered, disabled, deferred for future use) - Color Mode: dropdown (Grayscale/HSB/RGB/CMYK/Web Safe RGB). Changing
  the mode switches which slider group is visible via data-bind-visible
  on each slider container. The dialog change handler updates
  dialogState.color_mode and calls updateBindings().
- Color preview swatch: 40px square showing the current color via
  bind color="#dialog.hex"
- Sliders: one group per color mode, each containing rows of
  [label, slider, number_input]. Moving a slider updates
  dialogState via the dialog input handler, which recomputes the
  hex value from all components and calls updateBindings() to
  refresh the preview swatch and hex field.
- Hex field: 6-character hex string (RRGGBB, displayed with # prefix) - Bottom row: Preview checkbox (live document update, future),
  Cancel (dismiss_dialog), OK (swatch_options_confirm)

The dialog state includes working copies of all color components (h, s, b, r, g, bl, c, m, y, k, hex) and a _init_color field used during initialization. On open, the JS dialog init code detects _init_color and computes all component values from it using the color conversion functions (hsb_h, rgb_r, cmyk_c, etc.).

color_type.description: Color type (always Process Color for now)

color.description: Canonical color value; all components derive from this

h.set: fun v -> color <- hsb(v, hsb_s(color), hsb_b(color))

s.set: fun v -> color <- hsb(hsb_h(color), v, hsb_b(color))

b.set: fun v -> color <- hsb(hsb_h(color), hsb_s(color), v)

r.set: fun v -> color <- rgb(v, rgb_g(color), rgb_b(color))

g.set: fun v -> color <- rgb(rgb_r(color), v, rgb_b(color))

bl.set: fun v -> color <- rgb(rgb_r(color), rgb_g(color), v)

c.set: fun v -> color <- cmyk(v, cmyk_m(color), cmyk_y(color), cmyk_k(color))

m.set: fun v -> color <- cmyk(cmyk_c(color), v, cmyk_y(color), cmyk_k(color))

y.set: fun v -> color <- cmyk(cmyk_c(color), cmyk_m(color), v, cmyk_k(color))

k.set: fun v -> color <- cmyk(cmyk_c(color), cmyk_m(color), cmyk_y(color), v)

preview.description: When checked, color changes update the document live

arrow_alternates.description: Tool-alternate flyout menu shown on long-press (250ms) of the arrow selection tool button. Lists alternate selection tools: Partial Selection (select individual anchor points and path segments) and Interior Selection (select objects within groups without ungrouping). Clicking an item selects that tool and closes the dialog.

children.label: Partial Selection
children.summary: Select individual anchor points and path segments

pen_alternates.description: Tool-alternate flyout menu for the pen tool slot. Lists: Pen (draw paths by clicking anchor points), Add Anchor Point (add anchors to existing paths), Delete Anchor Point (remove anchors from paths), and Anchor Point (convert anchor point types between smooth and corner). Clicking selects the tool and closes the dialog.

children.label: Pen
children.summary: Draw paths by clicking to place anchor points

children.label: Add Anchor Point
children.summary: Add anchor points to an existing path

children.label: Delete Anchor Point
children.summary: Remove anchor points from a path

children.label: Anchor Point
children.summary: Convert anchor points between smooth and corner

pencil_alternates.description: Tool-alternate flyout menu for the pencil tool slot. Lists: Pencil (draw freehand paths), Path Eraser (erase portions of paths), and Smooth (smooth out rough path segments). Clicking selects the tool and closes the dialog.

text_alternates.description: Tool-alternate flyout menu for the type tool slot. Lists: Type (create and edit text) and Type on a Path (place text along a path curve). Clicking selects the tool and closes the dialog.

children.label: Type on a Path
children.summary: Place text along a path curve

shape_alternates.description: Tool-alternate flyout menu for the shape tool slot. Lists: Rectangle (draw rectangles and squares), Rounded Rectangle (draw rectangles with rounded corners), Polygon (draw regular polygons), and Star (draw star shapes). Clicking selects the tool and closes the dialog.

hand_alternates.description: Tool-alternate flyout menu shown on long-press (250ms) of the Hand tool button (row 4 col 0 of the tool grid). Lists the two navigation tools: Hand (pan the canvas by drag; spacebar pass-through) and Zoom (step / scrubby / marquee zoom). Clicking an item selects that tool and closes the dialog. Hand is the slot's primary; Zoom is the alternate. See HAND_TOOL.md and ZOOM_TOOL.md.

children.label: Zoom
children.summary: Zoom in by click, drag, or marquee

scale_alternates.description: Tool-alternate flyout menu shown on long-press (250ms) of the Scale tool button. Lists the Shear tool, the toolbar alternate of Scale. Clicking selects Shear and closes the dialog. Both tools share the transform-family conventions (state.transform_reference_point, dblclick-icon and Alt-click-canvas dialog gestures); the slot's primary tool is Scale. The Rotate tool lives in its own slot. See SCALE_TOOL.md and SHEAR_TOOL.md.

children.label: Shear
children.summary: Slant objects along a configurable axis

workspace_save_as.description: Text input dialog for naming a workspace layout. The Save button is disabled when the name is empty or matches the reserved name "Workspace". A warning message appears for the reserved name.

children.content: “Workspace” is reserved and cannot be used.

radio_field_row.description: Radio button + label + number input row for color picker HSB/RGB fields

cmyk_field_row.description: Label + number input + unit row for color picker CMYK fields

fill_stroke_widget.description: Overlapping fill/stroke color swatches with swap and reset buttons, plus Color/Gradient/None mode buttons at the bottom. All positions and sizes are derived from a single 'size' parameter.

slider_row.description: Labeled slider + number input row for color dialogs and panels

variation_widget.description:
Composite widget for a brush parameter that can vary along a stroke. Renders a base-value combo plus a variation-mode select; when the mode is 'random', two extra combos appear inline for min/max bounds (percent of base). When the mode is one of pressure / tilt / bearing / rotation, the base combo is inert (the live input drives the value at stroke time); the prior base value persists for round-tripping back to 'fixed' mode.
See transcripts/BRUSHES.md §Brush types and transcripts/BRUSH_OPTIONS_DIALOG.md §Variation widget.

simple_radial.name: Simple Radial
simple_radial.description: Plain two-stop radial gradients useful for highlights, glows, and soft backgrounds

spectrums.name: Spectrums
spectrums.description: Rainbow and spectrum gradients spanning the visible color range

default_brushes.name: Default Brushes
default_brushes.description: Starter brush library shipped with the application. Calligraphic seed for Phase 1; other brush types added as renderer support lands.
